question: A therapist tells a \(74\,\text{kg}\) patient with a broken leg that he must have his leg in a cast suspended horizontally. For minimum discomfort, the leg should be supported by a vertical strap attached at the center of mass of the leg-cast system. To comply with these instructions, the patient consults a table of typical mass distributions and finds that both upper legs (thighs) together typically account for \(21.5\%\) of body weight and the center of mass of each thigh is \(18.0\,\text{c...

Two identical, uniform beams weighing \(260\,\text{N}\) each are connected at one end by a frictionless hinge. A light horizontal crossbar attached at the midpoints of the beams maintains an angle of \(53.0^\circ\) between the beams. The beams are suspended from the ceiling by vertical wires. What force does the crossbar exert on each beam?
A spaceship flies past Earth. A crew member on board the spaceship measures its length, obtaining the value as shown in the figure. What length do observers measure on Earth?

The index of refraction for violet light in silica flint glass is 1.66, and that for red light is 1.62. What is the angular spread of visible light passing through a prism of apex angle \(60.0^\circ\) if the angle of incidence is \(50.0^\circ\)? See figure.

The figure shows the path of a light beam through several slabs with different indices of refraction. What is the minimum incident angle \(\theta_1\) needed to have total internal reflection at the surface between the medium with \(n = 1.20\) and the medium with \(n = 1.00\)?
Two spacecraft, A and B, are observed from the ground to be flying as shown in figure. What is the velocity of spacecraft A relative to spacecraft B?

In a shipping company distribution center, an open cart of mass 50.0 kg is rolling to the left at a speed of 5.00 m/s as shown in the figure. Ignore friction between the cart and the floor. A 15.0 kg package slides down a chute and leaves the end of the chute with a speed of 3.00 m/s. The package lands in the cart and they roll together. What is the final speed of the cart?

In the Figure, a \(6.00\,\text{m}\)-long, uniform beam is hanging from a point \(1.00\,\text{m}\) to the right of its center. The beam weighs \(140\,\text{N}\). At the right-hand end of the beam a weight is hung; an unknown weight \(w\) hangs at the left end. If the system is in equilibrium, what is \(w\)?

As shown in the figure, a plane glass plate with refractive index n = 1.50 is immersed in water. It is known that when a beam of light is incident on the water surface, the reflected light is completely polarized. If the reflected light from the surface of the glass is also to be completely polarized, what should be the angle θ between the glass surface and the horizontal plane (water surface)?

A box of negligible mass rests at the left end of a \( 2.00\, \text{m} \), \( 25.0\, \text{kg} \) plank. The sand is to be distributed uniformly throughout it. What mass of sand should be put into the box so that the plank balances horizontally on a fulcrum placed just below its midpoint?

A particle is confined between two impenetrable walls as shown in the figure. The wave function describing the particle's state is $\psi = cx(l-x)$, where $c$ is an undetermined constant. Calculate the probability of finding the particle in the interval from $0$ to $\frac{1}{3}l$.

Two protons are initially moving in opposite directions. They continue to exist after a head-on collision that also produces a neutral pion of mass as shown in the figure. If all three particles are at rest after the collision, find the initial speed of the protons. Energy is conserved in the collision.
A simple harmonic wave propagates along the positive x-axis. The waveform at $t_1 = 0$ and $t_2 = 0.25 \text{ s}$ is shown in the figure. Find the oscillation equation for point P.

The distance between the wave source S on the ground and the high-frequency wave detector D is d. The wave directly emitted from S and the wave emitted from S and reflected by a horizontal layer interfere constructively at D. The angle of reflection and the angle of incidence with the horizontal layer are the same. When the horizontal layer is gradually raised, no signal is detected at D. Neglect atmospheric absorption. Find the wavelength of the wave emitted by the wave source S.

A Fabry-Perot interferometer is composed of a pair of very smooth, parallel planar surfaces. The surfaces are coated with a film to reflect and transmit light. For light intensity incident perpendicularly on the film, the reflected portion is $R^2$, and the transmitted portion is $T^2$. As shown in the figure, light with intensity $I_0$ and wavelength $\lambda$ is incident from the left onto one of the surfaces. Part of the light is directly transmitted through the system, while other light i...

Two rods, $A$ and $B$, are undergoing uniform motion towards each other as shown in figure. An observer on rod $A$ finds that the left ends of the two rods coincide first, and after a time interval $\Delta t$, the right ends of the two rods coincide. What is the relative velocity of the two rods?
A one-euro coin is dropped from the Leaning Tower of Pisa and falls freely from rest. What is its position after \(3.0\,\text{s}\)? Ignore air resistance.

In the optical system shown in the figure, the focal length of the concave lens L1 is f1 = -20 cm, and the focal length of the convex lens L2 is f2 = 30 cm. What is the magnification if an object with its tip pointing upwards is located 20 cm to the left of L1 after passing through this system?
A spaceship moving away from the Earth fires a missile parallel to its direction of motion as shown in figure. What is the speed of the missile as measured by an observer on the Earth?

As shown in the figure, a lens system is composed of a converging lens $L_1$ and a diverging lens $L_2$. The entire optical system satisfies the paraxial approximation. If a paraxial object is located $60 \text{ cm}$ to the left of $L_1$, determine the image's lateral magnification.

A light ray traveling in air is incident on one face of a right-angle prism with index of refraction \( n = 1.50 \), as shown in the figure, and the ray follows the path shown in the figure. Assuming \( \theta = 60.0^\circ \) and that the base of the prism is mirrored, determine the angle \( \phi \) made by the outgoing ray with the normal to the right face of the prism.
Two identical objects are hung on two identical springs as shown in the figure. No swinging is allowed. How many normal modes are there?

A monochromatic point light source is placed on the left side of a convex lens as shown in the figure. Calculate the wavefront function of the light emitted from this point source after passing through the lens, at planes $\Sigma_2$ located before and after the focal point on the image side (right side) at a distance of 1 focal length. Assume the aperture of the lens is small, and the light waves satisfy the paraxial condition; the initial phase at the focal point can be set to $\varphi_0$.

A person is to be fitted with bifocals. She can see clearly when the object is between 30 cm and 1.5 m from the eye. The lower portions of the bifocals should enable her to see objects located 25 cm in front of the eye. What power should they have?

An observer in a coasting spacecraft moves toward a mirror at speed \( v = 0.650c \) relative to the reference frame labeled S in the figure. The mirror is stationary with respect to S. A light pulse emitted by the spacecraft travels toward the mirror and is reflected back to the spacecraft. The spacecraft is a distance \( d = 5.66 \times 10^{10} \) m from the mirror (as measured by observers in S) at the moment the light pulse leaves the spacecraft. What is the total travel time of the pulse...

As shown in Figure, a converging lens $L_1$ and a diverging lens $L_2$ are used to construct a camera lens. It is known that the focal length of the converging lens is $f_1 = 10 \text{ cm}$. It is required to form a 3 times magnified image on $P$. What is the distance from $L_2$ to the screen?

Two identical 15.0 kg balls, each 25.0 cm in diameter, are suspended by wires as shown in the figure. The entire apparatus is supported by a single wire, and the surfaces of the balls are perfectly smooth. How hard does each ball push on the other one?

In a version of the “Giant Swing”, the seat is connected to two cables, one of which is horizontal as shown in figure. The seat swings in a horizontal circle at a rate of \( 28.0 \) rpm (rev/min). If the seat weighs \( 255 \) N and an \( 825 \) N person is sitting in it, find the tension in the horizontal cable.

A retroreflector is an optical device that can reflect an incident light ray back along its original path. A retroreflector of the Sugden sphere type is made by silvering the rear hemisphere of a transparent spherical medium to create a reflective surface. By selecting an appropriate refractive index $n$, the incident light ray can be reflected back along its original path as shown in the figure. Determine the refractive index of the material of the sphere.
Analyze the RL circuit shown in the figure. What is \( V_\text{R} \) in the limit \( \omega \to 0 \)?

Four identical dipole radiators are arranged parallel to each other with equal spacing of 2.50 cm. Their excitation frequency is 3.00×10⁹ Hz. The phase modulation is as follows: starting from one end, the phase of each dipole lags the previous one by 90° as shown in the figure. Find the radiation intensity pattern far from the dipoles in the equatorial plane (perpendicular to the axis of the dipoles).

A pendulum with a cord of length \( r = 1.00 \text{ m} \) swings in a vertical plane as shown in the figure. When the pendulum is in the two horizontal positions \( \theta = 90.0^{\circ} \) and \( \theta = 270^{\circ} \), its speed is \( 5.00 \text{ m/s} \). Calculate the magnitude of the total acceleration at these positions.
A projectile is launched as shown in the figure. For a given \(v_0\), what value gives the maximum horizontal range?

A basketball player is standing on the floor as in figure. He shoots the ball at a angle with the horizontal from a height. At what speed must the player throw the basketball so that the ball goes through the hoop without striking the backboard?

In the ground reference frame (K frame), spaceship A (K' frame) and spaceship B are measured to have velocities of $V_A = -0.5c \, \hat{e}_x$ and $V_B = 0.5c \, \hat{e}_x$ respectively, and they are flying on parallel trajectories with a separation of $\Delta y = d$, as shown in Figure. When the two spaceships reach $(x_A, y_A) = (0,0)$ and $(x_B, y_B) = (0, d)$ respectively at $t = 0$, spaceship A emits a small package with a velocity of $v = 0.75c$ in the K frame, which is then intercepted...

Suppose there are two point sources, $S_1$ and $S_2$, with a separation of $t$. The receiving screen is placed perpendicular to the line connecting $S_1$ and $S_2$ as shown in the figure, and the foot of the perpendicular is the origin of the receiving screen. The distance from the receiving screen to the midpoint of $S_1$ and $S_2$ is $D$, and $D \gg t, D \gg x$. Find the distance from the $j$-th bright fringe to the origin of the screen.

The mechanism shown in figure is used to raise a crate of supplies from a ship's hold. The crate has total mass \(50\text{ kg}\). A rope is wrapped around a wooden cylinder that turns on a metal axle. The cylinder has radius \(0.25\text{ m}\) and moment of inertia \(I = 2.9\text{ kg}\cdot\text{m}^2\) about the axle. The crate is suspended from the free end of the rope. One end of the axle pivots on frictionless bearings; a crank handle is attached to the other end. When the crank is turned, t...

A blue car of length \(4.52\ \text{m}\) is moving north on a roadway that intersects another perpendicular roadway as shown in the figure. The blue car has a constant acceleration of magnitude \(2.10\ \text{m/s}^2\) directed south. The time interval required for the nose of the blue car to move from the near (south) edge of the intersection to the north edge of the intersection is \(3.10\ \text{s}\). How far is the nose of the blue car from the south edge of the intersection when it stops?

A rocket ship travels from Earth to a space station. The space station is at rest relative to the Earth, and the distance between them is $9.0 \times 10^9 \, \text{m}$. The clocks on Earth and the space station are synchronized as shown in figure. When the rocket ship passes Earth, the astronauts synchronize the clock on the rocket ship to the same reading as the clock on Earth. When the rocket ship passes the space station, the astronauts find that the clock on the rocket ship is slow by $3...

As shown in Figure, two containers with the same volume ($V_1 = V_2 = V$) are connected by a thin tube. The two containers are maintained at constant temperatures $T_1$ and $T_2$ respectively. All gas molecules have the same mass $m$. Find the ratio of the gas pressures in the two containers as a function of $T_1$ and $T_2$, i.e., $P_1/P_2 = f(T_1, T_2)$.

A parallel monochromatic light with a wavelength $\lambda$ of 0.5 μm is perpendicularly incident on a double-slit plane as shown in figure. The distance between the two slits, $t$, is known to be 0.5 mm. A thin ideal lens L with a focal length $f'$ of 5 cm is placed 5 cm away on the other side of the double slits. A receiving screen is placed at the image focal plane of L. What is the fringe spacing?

A long straight wire carries a current $I_1$. A rectangular loop has length $l$ and width $w$, and carries a current $I_2$. The wire is in the plane of the loop and is parallel to the long sides of the loop, as shown in the figure. Find the force on the wire, $F_{\text{wire}}$.

A nucleus at rest undergoes a decay emitting an $\alpha$-particle of de-Broglie wavelength, $\lambda = 5.76 \times 10^{-15} \text{ m}$. If the mass of the daughter nucleus is $223.610 \text{ amu}$ and that of the $\alpha$-particle is $4.002 \text{ amu}$. Determine the total kinetic energy in the final state.

A beam of thermal neutrons emerges from a nuclear reactor and is incident on a crystal as shown in the figure. The beam is Bragg scattered from a crystal whose scattering planes are separated by \(0.247\ \text{nm}\). From the continuous energy spectrum of the beam we wish to select neutrons of energy \(0.0105\ \text{eV}\). Find the Bragg-scattering angle that results in a scattered beam of this energy.

The scanning function of a television picture tube is achieved by deflecting the electron beam using a magnetic field. The magnetic field comes from a set of deflection coils placed around the neck of the picture tube. The electron beam is emitted from the electron gun, with an energy of approximately 3.0 keV (before deflection). After deflection, the electrons are further accelerated. The dimensions of each part are shown in Figure. At maximum deflection, estimate the magnitude of the magnet...

final_category_zh: 近代物理、量子与相对论
question: Nuclei of a radioactive element $A$ are being produced at a constant rate $\alpha$. The element has a decay constant $\lambda$. At time $t = 0$, there are $N_0$ nuclei of the element as shown in figure. Calculate the number $N$ of nuclei of $A$ at time $t$

A small remote-controlled car with mass \(1.60\) kg moves at a constant speed of \(v = 12.0\) m/s in a track formed by a vertical circle inside a hollow metal cylinder as shown in the figure. What is the magnitude of the normal force exerted on the car by the walls of the cylinder at point \(B\) (top of the track)?

A machine part as shown in figure consists of three small disks linked by lightweight struts. What is the body's kinetic energy if it rotates about axis 1 with angular speed \( \omega = 4.0 \text{ rad/s} \)?

In the circuit shown in Figure, the switch is initially at point A. At time $t = 0$, the switch is moved to point B. After a long time, If there is voltage remaining on the capacitor, what are the voltages $V_1$

A disk of radius 25.0 cm is free to turn about an axle perpendicular to it through its center. It has a very thin but strong string wrapped around its rim as shown in the figure. The pull increases in magnitude and produces an acceleration of the ball that obeys the equation \(a(t) = At\), where \(t\) is in seconds and \(A\) is a constant. The cylinder starts from rest, and at the end of the third second, the ball’s acceleration is \(1.80\text{ m/s}^2\). Through what angle has the disk turned...

A spaceship is flying away from a receiving station along the line connecting the spaceship and the receiving station as shown in figure. The light source on the spaceship emits light pulses with a period of $T_0 = 4 \text{ s}$. Find the period of the pulses received by the receiving station on the ground.
The graph in figure was measured in a photoelectric-effect experiment. What experimental value of Planck’s constant is obtained from these data?

Figure shows a loop with resistance \(0.10\,\Omega\) around a solenoid. The solenoid is \(10\,\text{cm}\) long, has \(100\) turns, and carries the current shown in the graph. Positive current is cw when seen from the left. Find the current in the loop at \(t = 2.5\,\text{s}\).
As shown in the figure, parallel monochromatic light with wavelength $\lambda$ is obliquely incident at a small angle $\alpha$ on a double slit. Find the position of the zero-order bright fringe.

The “Giant Swing” at a county fair consists of a vertical central shaft with a number of horizontal arms attached at its upper end. Each arm supports a seat suspended from a cable, and the upper end of the cable is fastened to the arm at a point as shown in the figure. Find the time of one revolution of the swing if the cable supporting a seat makes an angle of \(30.0^\circ\) with the vertical.

Two locations, B and A as shown in figure. At location A, there was a factory power outage due to overload. On the same day, at location B, there was a collision between a bicycle and a truck. Calculate the time interval between these two events observed in a spaceship traveling at $u = 0.8c$ from A to B.

In a Young's interference experiment setup (as shown in Figure), a monochromatic light source with a wavelength of $\lambda = 0.55 \, \mu\text{m}$ is used as the illuminating light source, the slit separation is $d = 3.3 \, \text{mm}$, and the distance from the slits to the screen is $L = 3 \, \text{m}$. Calculate the fringe spacing $\Delta x$.

An optical system is composed of a positive lens $L_1$ with $f'_1 = 5 \, \text{cm}$ and a negative lens $L_2$ with $f'_2 = -10 \, \text{cm}$. $L_2$ is located $5 \, \text{cm}$ to the right of $L_1$. Calculate the focal length $f'$
The magnetic field in the figure is decreasing at the rate \(0.10\ \text{T/s}\). What is the acceleration of a proton initially at rest at point c?

A block is attached to a very light horizontal spring of force constant 500.0 N/m and is resting on a frictionless horizontal table as shown in figure. Suddenly it is struck by a stone traveling horizontally to the right, whereupon the stone rebounds at 2.00 m/s horizontally to the left. Find the maximum distance that the block will compress the spring after the collision.

A 1500 kg rocket is to be launched with an initial upward speed of 50.0 m/s. In order to assist its engines, the engineers will start it from rest on a ramp that rises 53° above the horizontal, as shown in the figure. At the bottom, the ramp turns upward and launches the rocket vertically. The engines provide a constant forward thrust of 2000 N, and friction with the ramp surface is a constant 500 N. How far from the base of the ramp should the rocket start, as measured along the surface of t...

The two mirrors illustrated in the figure meet at a right angle. The beam of light in the vertical plane indicated by the dashed lines strikes mirror 1 as shown. Determine the distance the reflected light beam travels before striking mirror 2.

A chunk of ice is sliding on the floor of an ice-covered valley when it collides with and sticks to another chunk of ice that is initially at rest, as shown in the figure. Since the valley is icy, there is no friction. After the collision, how high above the valley floor will the combined chunks go?

An optical fiber has an index of refraction \( n \) and diameter \( d \). It is surrounded by vacuum. Light is sent into the fiber along its axis as shown in figure. Find the smallest outside radius \( R_{\text{min}} \) permitted for a bend in the fiber if no light is to escape.

The truck in figure is moving at a speed of \(10.0 \, \text{m/s}\) relative to the ground. The person on the truck throws a baseball in the backward direction at a speed of \(20.0 \, \text{m/s}\) relative to the truck. What is the velocity of the baseball as measured by the observer on the ground?
In figure the weight \( w \) is \( 60.0 \) N. Find the magnitudes of the horizontal forces \( \vec{F}_2 \) that must be applied to hold the system in the position shown.

A beam of light both reflects and refracts at the surface between air and glass as shown in the figure. If the refractive index of the glass is \(n_g\), find the angle of incidence \(\theta_1\) in air that would result in the reflected ray and the refracted ray being perpendicular to each other.
As shown in Figure, consider a dual-lens system. A 1 cm high object is located on the left of the convex lens. Calculate the size and position of the image.

You are lowering two boxes down a ramp by pulling on a rope parallel to the surface of the ramp as shown in figure. Both boxes move together at a constant speed of \( 15.0 \) cm/s. The coefficient of kinetic friction between the ramp and the lower box is \( 0.444 \), and the coefficient of static friction between the two boxes is \( 0.800 \). What is the magnitude of the friction force on the upper box?

A thin-walled, hollow spherical shell of mass \(m\) and radius \(r\) starts from rest and rolls without slipping down a track as shown in the figure. Points \(A\) and \(B\) are on a circular part of the track, and the work done by rolling friction is negligible. How hard does the track push on the shell at point \(B\), which is at the same level as the center of the circle?

A NPN transistor in a common emitter mode is used as a simple voltage amplifier with a collector current of $4 \text{ mA}$. The terminal of an $8 \text{V}$ battery is connected to the collector through a load resistance $R_L$ and to the base through a resistance $R_B$ as shown in figure. The collector emitter voltage $V_{CE} = 4 \text{ V}$, base emitter voltage $V_{BE} = 0.6 \text{ V}$ and current amplification factor is $\beta = 100$. Calculate the values of $R_B$.

A triangular glass prism with apex angle \( \Phi = 60.0^\circ \) has an index of refraction \( n = 1.50 \) as shown in the figure. What is the smallest angle of incidence \( \theta_1 \) for which a light ray can emerge from the other side?

An observer at rest sees a plane electromagnetic wave $E_0e^{i(kx-\omega t)}$ perpendicularly incident on the surface of a medium with refractive index $n$, as shown in the figure. The surface moves along the direction of the surface normal with velocity $v$ in the same direction as the medium. Find the frequency of the reflected wave.

The structure of an optical system is shown in the figure. The focal length of thin lens $L_1$ is $f'_1 = 3\,\text{cm}$, and the focal length of thin lens $L_2$ is $f'_2 = -3\,\text{cm}$. A photographic plate, $L$, is placed at the image plane $P$. The positions of $L_1$ and $P$ are fixed. The distance between $L_1$ and $L_2$ is $d$. Adjust $d$ so that both objects at infinity and nearby objects can be imaged on the photographic plate. When the object moves from infinity to a distance of $100...

Figure is a model of a benzene molecule. All atoms lie in a plane, and the carbon atoms (\( m_{\text{C}} = 1.99 \times 10^{-26} \text{ kg} \)) form a regular hexagon, as do the hydrogen atoms (\( m_{\text{H}} = 1.67 \times 10^{-27} \text{ kg} \)). Calculate the moment of inertia of the molecule about an axis perpendicular to the plane of the paper through the center point \( O \).
Two blocks connected by a cord passing over a small, frictionless pulley rest on frictionless planes as shown in figure. What is the tension in the cord?

As shown in the figure, two coherent point sources S1 and S2 produce interference fringes on a receiving screen 2 m away. Now, a convex lens L with a focal length of 50 cm is placed between the sources and the screen, and S1 and S2 are on the focal plane of L. Find by what factor the fringe spacing changes.

James and Ramon are apart on a frozen pond. Midway between them is a mug of their favorite beverage as shown in figure. They pull on the ends of a light rope stretched between them. When James has moved 6.0 m toward the mug, how far has Ramon moved?

In a Lloyd's mirror experiment, as shown in the figure, the distance from the light source to the receiving screen is 1.5 m, and the vertical distance from the light source to the Lloyd's mirror surface is 2 mm. If the wavelength of light is \(\lambda=500\,\text{nm}\), find the distance from the 1st bright fringe to the center of the screen.

A 2.8 kg block slides over the smooth, icy hill shown in the figure. The top of the hill is horizontal. What minimum speed must the block have at the base of the 70 m hill to pass over the pit at the far (right-hand) side of that hill?

A closed, square loop is formed with 40 cm of wire having \( R = 0.10 \, \Omega \), as shown in figure. A 0.50 T magnetic field is perpendicular to the loop. At \( t = 0 \, \text{s} \), two diagonally opposite corners of the loop begin to move apart. How long does it take the loop to collapse to a straight line?

A square metal plate is pivoted about an axis through point \(O\) at its center and perpendicular to the plate as shown in the figure. Calculate the net torque about this axis due to the three forces shown in the figure if the magnitudes of the forces are \(F_1 = 18.0\text{ N}\), \(F_2 = 26.0\text{ N}\), and \(F_3 = 14.0\text{ N}\). The plate and all forces are in the plane of the page.

Figure shows an overhead view of a room of square floor area. At the center of the room is a mirror set in a vertical plane and rotating on a vertical shaft about an axis coming out of the page. A bright red laser beam enters from the center point on one wall of the room and strikes the mirror. As the mirror rotates, the reflected laser beam creates a red spot sweeping across the walls of the room. In what time interval does the spot change from its minimum to its maximum speed?
The light beam in figure strikes surface 2 at the critical angle. Determine the angle of incidence \(\theta_1\).

As shown in the figure, three simple harmonic waves with the same frequency and the same oscillation direction (perpendicular to the plane of the paper) meet at point P during propagation. The oscillation equations for the three simple harmonic waves alone at S1, S2, and S3 are respectively: $y_1 = A \cos(\omega t + \frac{\pi}{2})$, $y_2 = A \cos(\omega t)$, $y_3 = A \cos(\omega t - \frac{\pi}{2})$. If $S_2P = 4\lambda$, $S_1P = S_3P = 5\lambda$ (where $\lambda$ is the wavelength), find the e...
In the laboratory frame, two electrons in a collision machine fly toward each other as shown in the figure. What is their relative speed when they collide?

Block \( B \), with mass 5.00 kg, and block \( A \), with mass 8.00 kg, which in turn is on a horizontal tabletop as shown in the figure. There is no friction between block \( A \) and the tabletop, but the coefficient of static friction between blocks \( A \) and \( B \) is 0.750. A light string attached to block \( A \) passes over a frictionless, massless pulley, and block \( C \) is suspended from the other end of the string. What is the largest mass that block \( C \) can have so that bl...

A firefighter, a distance from a burning building, directs a stream of water from a fire hose as shown in figure. If the initial speed of the stream is \( v_i \), at what height \( h \) does the water strike the building?

A point light source is located behind a transparent sphere. The paraxial light rays emitted from the front surface of the sphere are parallel as shown in figure. Find the refractive index of the transparent sphere material.

Two vertical antennas are placed as shown in Figure. The two antennas are excited in phase. Determine the radiation intensity observed in direction E, W shown in the figure. It is known that when one antenna is excited individually, the radiation intensity in all horizontal directions is $I_0$, and when the other antenna is excited, the radiation intensity in all horizontal directions is $2I_0$.
Figure shows a frustum of a cone. Calculate the area of the curved surface.

An iceboat is at rest on a frictionless horizontal surface as shown in figure. Due to the blowing wind, 4.0 s after the iceboat is released, it is moving to the right at 6.0 m/s (about 22 km/h, or 13 mi/h). What constant horizontal force \( F_{\mathrm{W}} \) does the wind exert on the iceboat? The combined mass of iceboat and rider is 200 kg.

Two ropes are connected to a steel cable that supports a hanging weight, as shown in the figure. If the maximum tension either rope can sustain without breaking is 5000 N, determine the maximum value of the hanging weight that these ropes can safely support. Ignore the weight of the ropes and of the steel cable.

As shown in the figure, one end of a light spring is fixed, and the other end is attached to a light string. The light string passes over a pulley and is connected to an object. The string does not slip on the pulley, allowing the object to oscillate freely up and down. The moment of inertia of the pulley is $J$. Find the period of oscillation of the object.

Consider a potential well defined as \( U(x) = \infty \) for \( x < 0 \), \( U(x) = 0 \) for \( 0 < x < L \), and \( U(x) = U_0 > 0 \) for \( x > L \) as shown in the figure. Consider a particle with mass \( m \) and kinetic energy \( E < U_0 \) that is trapped in the well. The wave function must remain finite as \( x \to \infty \). What must the form of the function \( \psi(x) \) for \( x > L \) be in order to satisfy both the Schrödinger equation and this boundary condition at infinity?

An insulated cylinder has a movable, frictionless piston. The piston has area $A$. Initially, the gas volume is $V_1$, containing $N$ grams of helium gas, as shown in Figure 32-1. The external pressure is $P$. The gas is heated very slowly by an internal heating coil until the gas volume reaches $2V_1$. Find the change in the internal energy of the gas, $\Delta U$.

As shown in Figure, the optical system is composed of a collimator, a stage, and a telescope. It is known that the focal length of the telescope objective $L_o$ is 16.00 cm. A reticle with a crosshair and a bright cross is placed at the focal plane P of $L_o$, as shown in Figure 2.111. A double-sided parallel plane mirror M is placed on the stage. When observed through the eyepiece $L_e$ of the telescope, the crosshair on the reticle and the cross object can be seen clearly after refraction b...
Consider a "crystal" consisting of two fixed ions of charge \( +e \) and two electrons as shown in figure. Find the value of \( d \) for which the total energy is a minimum.

As shown in the figure, P is a photosensitive plate, M is a reflecting mirror, and the wavelength of the incident parallel light is $\lambda$. Calculate the fringe spacing formed by the incident light on the photosensitive plate.

A surveyor measures the distance across a straight river by the following method as shown in figure. Starting directly across from a tree on the opposite bank, she walks \( d = 100 \\text{ m} \) along the riverbank to establish a baseline. Then she sights across to the tree. The angle from her baseline to the tree is \( \theta = 35.0^\circ \). How wide is the river?

As shown in Figure, a microlens made of variable refractive index material is used to focus a parallel beam of light. Its refractive index is distributed symmetrically with respect to the radius. It is known that its focal length $f$ is much greater than the lens aperture $a$, and $a$ is also much greater than the thickness $d$. Quantitatively derive the function $n(r)$, assuming the on-axis refractive index is $n_0$.

Assuming that the difference between Thomson's calculated \( e/m \) in his second experiment (see Figure 3-19) and the currently accepted value was due entirely to his neglecting the horizontal component of Earth's magnetic field outside the deflection plates, what value for that component does the difference imply? (Thomson's data: \( B = 5.5 \times 10^{-4} \) T, \( \mathcal{E} = 1.5 \times 10^4 \) V/m, \( x_1 = 5 \) cm, \( y_2/x_2 = 8/110 \).)

A block slides down a surface to the horizontal as shown in the figure. The coefficient of kinetic friction is \(0.25\). A string attached to the block is wrapped around a flywheel on a fixed axis at \(O\). The flywheel has mass \(25.0\text{ kg}\) and moment of inertia \(0.500\text{ kg}\cdot\text{m}^2\) with respect to the axis of rotation. The string pulls without slipping at a perpendicular distance of \(0.200\text{ m}\) from that axis. What is the tension in the string?

Block A in figure weighs \(60.0\ \text{N}\). The coefficient of static friction between the block and the surface on which it rests is 0.25. The weight \(w\) is \(12.0\ \text{N}\), and the system is in equilibrium. Find the maximum weight \(w\) for which the system will remain in equilibrium.

On a horizontal table, two small balls A and B, both with mass $m$, are connected by a light spring, as shown in the figure. The two balls are pulled apart in opposite directions along the axis of the spring and then released. Find the angular frequency $\omega$ of the oscillation of the two balls thereafter.

A worker wants to turn over a uniform, \(1250\,\text{N}\), rectangular crate by pulling on one of its vertical sides. The floor is rough enough to prevent the crate from slipping. What is the minimum coefficient of static friction needed to prevent the crate from slipping on the floor?

Two asteroids of equal mass in the asteroid belt between Mars and Jupiter collide with a glancing blow. Asteroid \( A \) is deflected from its original direction, while asteroid \( B \), which was initially at rest, travels as shown in the figure. What fraction of the original kinetic energy of asteroid A dissipates during this collision?

As shown in the figure, a beam of light originally converging towards point $P$ passes through a glass plate before reaching point $P$. The glass plate is perpendicular to the beam's axis. By how much does it move?

A car of length $l_0$ at rest is moving with velocity $v$ relative to the ground as shown in the figure. A small ball is ejected forward from the rear wall of the car with velocity $u_0$. Find the time it takes for the ball to travel from the rear wall to the front wall of the car as observed from the ground.

A 45.0 kg woman stands up in a 60.0 kg canoe 5.00 m long. She walks from a point from one end to a point from the other end as shown in the figure. If you ignore resistance to motion of the canoe in the water, how far does the canoe move during this process?
A ray of light travels into another medium, making an angle of \( \theta_1 = 45.0^{\circ} \) with the normal as in figure. Find the angle of refraction \( \theta_2 \) if the second medium is water.

When the light ray illustrated in figure passes through the glass block of index of refraction \( n = 1.50 \), it is shifted laterally by the distance \( d \). Find the time interval required for the light to pass through the glass block.

There is a glass slab between the eye E and the object PQ, as shown in the figure. The thickness $d$ of the slab is 30 cm. Find the distance between the image of object PQ and the object (the surrounding medium of the slab is air).
A motorcyclist heading east through a small town accelerates after he leaves the city limits as shown in figure. Where is he when his speed is \(25\,\text{m/s}\)?

The boom shown in Figure weighs \(2600\,\text{N}\) and is attached to a frictionless pivot at its lower end. It is not uniform; the distance of its center of gravity from the pivot is \(35\%\) of its length. Find the vertical component of the force exerted on the boom at its lower end.

The construction of a simple musical instrument is as follows: A string of negligible mass is tightly stretched between two fixed points. A mass is attached at a distance from one end, as shown in Figure. The object is pulled away from its equilibrium position by a small distance A ($A \ll x, A \ll l-x$), and then vibrates. Calculate the frequency of the sound wave, ν.
Rockets A and B move in opposite directions as viewed from Earth as shown in figure. What is the velocity of rocket B relative to rocket A?

In figure a spider is resting after starting to spin its web. The gravitational force on the spider makes it exert a downward force of \( 0.150 \text{ N} \) on the junction of the three strands of silk. The junction is supported by different tension forces in the two strands above it so that the resultant force on the junction is zero. The two sloping strands are perpendicular. The tension \( T_x \) is \( 0.127 \text{ N} \). Find the tension \( T_y \

Two objects, A and B, are connected by hinges to a rigid rod. The objects slide along perpendicular guide rails as shown in the figure. Assume object A slides to the left with a constant speed. Find the velocity \(v_{\text{B}}\) of object B as a function of the angle \(\theta\).

As shown in the figure, a light ray is incident between mirrors and reflects n times, finally returning along its original path of incidence. Write out the expression for the relationship between the initial angle of incidence θi and α.
As shown in Figure, the focal lengths of a biconvex thick lens for the front and rear refracting spherical surfaces are $f_1$, $f'_1$ and $f_2$, $f'_2$ respectively. Try to find the focal length.
answer: \( \frac{nn_L}{n_L - n} \cdot \frac{r_1 r_2}{(n_L - n)d - n_L r_1 + n_L r_2} \)

question: The energy-level scheme for the hypothetical one-electron element Searsium is shown in the figure. The potential energy is taken to be zero for an electron at an infinite distance from the nucleus. Photons emitted in the Searsium transitions \(n = 3 \to n = 2\) and \(n = 3 \to n = 1\) will eject photoelectrons from an unknown metal, but the photon emitted from the transition \(n = 4 \to n = 3\) will not. What is the maximum work function of the metal?

When a certain photoelectric surface is illuminated with light of different wavelengths, the following stopping potentials are observed as shown in the figure. Plot the stopping potential on the vertical axis against the frequency of the light on the horizontal axis. Determine the value of Planck's constant \( h \) (assuming that the value of \( e \) is known).

A train car is moving at a high speed relative to the ground as shown in the figure. Inside the car, an observer sees passengers A at the front end and B at the rear end of the car shooting at each other. Passenger A fires first, leading by a time of $10^{-8}\ \text{s}$. What is the time interval between the two shots for an observer on the ground?

Consider the system shown in figure. Block \( A \) weighs \( 45.0 \) N, and block \( B \) weighs \( 25.0 \) N. Once block \( B \) is set into downward motion, it descends at a constant speed. A cat, also of weight \( 45.0 \) N, falls asleep on top of block \( A \). If block \( B \) is now set into downward motion, what is its acceleration?

As shown in Figure, a vertically oriented dipole is suspended from a radio sounding balloon. If the total power of the transmitter is 0.5 W, calculate the power density P at the receiver in units of W m$^{-2}$.

As shown in the figure, monochromatic light (\(\lambda_0 = 550\) nm) emitted from point S is incident from air (\(n_1=1\)) into a certain transparent medium (\(n_2=1.55\)), and then refracted back into air. If the upper and lower surfaces of the transparent medium are parallel to each other, with thickness \(e=1.0\) cm, the angle of incidence is \(i_1=30^\circ\), and \(|SA|=|BC|=5\) cm. Find the angle of refraction \(i_2\).
A conducting sphere, as shown in the figure, with uniform magnetization is rotating about its $z$-axis with low speed. Find the potential difference between the equator and the pole.

Figure shows the wave function of a particle. The wave function is zero outside this region. Calculate the probability of finding the particle in the interval \(-2.0\ \text{mm} \leq x \leq 2.0\ \text{mm}\).

A rigid wire is bent into a semicircle, and it rotates in a uniform magnetic field, as shown in Figure. If the internal resistance of the instrument M is $M_R$, and other resistances are negligible. Find the amplitude $I_0$ of the induced current.

To loosen a pipe fitting, a plumber slips a piece of scrap pipe (a "cheater") over his wrench handle. He stands on the end of the cheater as shown in the figure. Find the magnitude of the torque he applies about the center of the fitting.

Two events occur at the same location on the ground, with a time interval as shown in figure. An observer in a high-speed rocket finds that the time interval between the two events is 8 seconds, and the order does not change. What is the speed of this rocket?

Four vertical dipoles are placed at the vertices of a horizontal rectangle with side lengths $a$ and $b$, as shown in Figure. If they are excited in phase with a radiation wavelength of $\lambda$, what are the smallest possible positive values for $b$ in order to produce maximum intensity in the direction $\theta=30^\circ$ at a large distance?

Engineers are designing a system by which a falling imparts kinetic energy to a rotating uniform drum to which it is attached by thin, very light wire wrapped around the rim of the drum as shown in the figure. There is no appreciable friction in the axle of the drum, and everything starts from rest. This system is being tested on Earth, but it is to be used on Mars, where the acceleration due to gravity is \(3.71\text{ m/s}^2\). In the Earth tests, when \(m\) is set to \(15.0\text{ kg}\) and...
What is \( V_\text{C} \) if the emf frequency in figure is \( 10\,\text{kHz} \)?

As shown in Figure, $OO'$ is the axis of symmetry of the double-wedge prism interference setup. A parallel light beam is incident on the double-wedge prism from the left, parallel to the $OO'$ direction. Interference fringes with a fringe spacing of $\Delta x = 0.5 \text{mm}$ are obtained on a screen placed at a certain distance to the right of the double-wedge prism. Given that the apex angle $\alpha$ of the double-wedge prism is very small and the refractive index $n = 1.5$, find $\alpha$ i...

Two "identical" objects with constant heat capacity (as $C_p$), with initial temperatures $T_1$ and $T_2$ respectively, are used as heat sources for a Carnot engine (operating in an infinitesimally small reversible cycle), as shown in the figure. If the pressure of the two objects remains constant and no phase change occurs, find the total work $W$ done by the heat engine.

Consider the system shown in the figure. The rope and pulley have negligible mass, and the pulley is frictionless. Initially the block is moving downward and the other block is moving to the right, both with a speed of 0.900 m/s. The blocks come to rest after moving 2.00 m. Calculate the coefficient of kinetic friction between the 8.00 kg block and the tabletop.

As shown in Figure, $L_1$ is a thin convex lens, and $Q$ is a 2.00 cm tall linear object placed perpendicular to the optical axis. It is known that $Q$ forms an image through $L_1$, and the image distance is 40.0 cm. Now, in order from right to left of $L_1$, thin convex lenses $L_2$, $L_3$, $L_4$ and screen $P$ are placed, and the distances between them are as shown in the figure. All lenses are coaxial, and the screen is perpendicular to the optical axis. The magnitude of the focal length o...

Block \( A \) in the figure weighs \( 1.90 \, \text{N} \), and block \( B \) weighs \( 4.20 \, \text{N} \). The coefficient of kinetic friction between all surfaces is 0.30. Find the magnitude of the horizontal force \( \vec{F} \) necessary to drag block \( B \) to the left at constant speed if \( A \) and \( B \) are connected by a light, flexible cord passing around a fixed, frictionless pulley.

As shown in the figure, two rollers with parallel axes are placed. A uniform wooden plank of mass m is placed on the two rollers. The coefficient of friction between the plank and both rollers is μ. If it undergoes simple harmonic motion, what is its period?

You push a food tray through the cafeteria line with a constant force. The tray pushes a milk carton as shown in the figure. The tray and carton slide on a horizontal surface so greasy that friction can be ignored. Find the horizontal force that the tray exerts on the carton.

A photon with an energy of $4.9 \text{ eV}$ ejects photoelectrons from tungsten as shown in the figure. When the ejected electron enters a constant magnetic field of strength $B = 2.5 \text{ mT}$ at an angle with the field direction, the maximum pitch of the helix described by the electron is found to be $2.7 \text{ mm}$. Find the work function of the metal in electron volts. Given that the specific charge of electron is $1.76 \times 10^{11} \text{ C/kg}$.

The square loop shown in figure moves into a magnetic field at a constant speed. The loop has a resistance of \( 0.10 \, \Omega \), and it enters the field at \( t = 0 \, \text{s} \). What is the maximum current?

One drop of oil falls straight down onto the road from the engine of a moving car every \( 5 \text{ s} \). Figure shows the pattern of the drops left behind on the pavement. What is the average speed of the car over this section of its motion?

A parallel plate capacitor of capacitance $100\,\mu\text{F}$ and separation $1\,\text{cm}$ is charged with a battery to a potential difference of $10\,\text{V}$. The battery is then disconnected. An electromagnetic wave is now incident on the negatively charged plate, which emits electrons with kinetic energies ranging from $0$ to $1.5\,\text{eV}$. The electrons are attracted to the positive plate. The current flowing between the two plates varies with time $t$ as shown in the figure. What is...

As shown in Figure, two monochromatic waves with a wavelength of 500 nm travel from point A to point B. One of the waves passes through a water-filled glass cup, with the glass cup wall thickness being 0.5 cm and the inner distance being 10 cm. Assuming the two waves are in phase at A, find the phase difference at B. The refractive indices of the glass and water are 1.52 and 1.33, respectively.

A helium--neon laser can produce a green laser beam instead of a red one. The figure shows the transitions involved to form the red beam and the green beam. After a population inversion is established, neon atoms make a variety of downward transitions in falling from the state labeled \(E_4^*\) down eventually to level \(E_1\) (arbitrarily assigned the energy \(E_1 = 0\)). The atoms emit both red light with a wavelength of 632.8 nm in a transition \(E_4^* - E_3\) and green light with a wavele...

In the circuit shown in the figure, the switch is initially closed, with a steady current $I = V_0/R$ flowing through. At time $t = 0$, switch $S$ is suddenly opened. Find the maximum voltage $V_{\text{max}}$ observed across the capacitor.

A small rock with mass 0.20 kg is released from rest at point A, which is at the top edge of a large, hemispherical bowl with radius \( R = 0.50 \) m as shown in the figure. Assume that the size of the rock is small compared to \( R \), so that the rock can be treated as a particle, and assume that the rock slides rather than rolls. The work done by friction on the rock when it moves from point A to point B at the bottom of the bowl has magnitude 0.22 J. Between points A and B, how much work...
The thin lens shown in the figure has surface radii of 15.00 cm (left) and 10.00 cm (right). The refractive index of the glass is 1.5. Calculate the focal length of this lens.

As shown in Figure, a photon with wavelength $\lambda = 3 \text{ Å}$ is scattered by a free electron initially at rest at an angle of $90^\circ$. Find the kinetic energy $T$ of the recoiling electron.

An alpha particle approaches a nucleus with a speed of \(1.50 \times 10^7\,\text{m/s}\). As the figure shows, the alpha particle is scattered at the slower speed of \(1.49 \times 10^7\,\text{m/s}\). At what speed does the \(^{197}\text{Au}\) nucleus recoil?

A small bead can slide without friction on a circular hoop that is in a vertical plane. The hoop rotates at a constant rate of \(4.00\,\text{rev/s}\) about a vertical diameter as shown in the figure. Find the angle \(\beta\) at which the bead is in vertical equilibrium. (It has a radial acceleration toward the axis.)
The switch in figure has been open for a long time. It is closed at \( t = 0 \, \text{s} \). What is the current through the battery after the switch has been closed a long time?
Figure shows the wave function of an electron in a rigid box. The electron energy is \(6.0\ \text{eV}\). How long is the box?

The structure and working principle of a Rayleigh interferometer are as follows (as shown in Figure): A sodium vapor lamp is used as the light source, placed in front of lens $L_1$. The changes in interference fringes are observed on the focal plane of lens $L_2$. Two identical glass tubes, $T_1$ and $T_2$, are placed between the two lenses. At the beginning of the experiment, $T_2$ is filled with air, and $T_1$ is evacuated to a vacuum. At this time, the interference fringes are observed. Th...

A glider sits on a frictionless, horizontal air track, connected to a spring. You pull on the glider, stretching the spring, and release it from rest as shown in figure. The glider moves back toward its equilibrium position (\( x = 0 \)). What is its \( x \)-velocity when \( x = 0.080 \) m?

Figure shows a ballistic pendulum, a simple system for measuring the speed of a bullet. A bullet makes a completely inelastic collision with a block of wood, which is suspended like a pendulum. After the impact, the block swings up to a maximum height. What is the initial speed \( v_1 \) of the bullet?

When the system rotates about the axis of the rod, the strings are extended as shown in the figure, and the tension in the upper string is \(80.0\,\text{N}\). Find the number of revolutions per minute at which the lower cord just goes slack.
A sled with rider having a combined mass of 125 kg travels over a perfectly smooth icy hill as shown in figure. How far does the sled land from the foot of the cliff?

An observer to the right of the mirror-lens combination shown in the figure (not to scale) sees two real images that are the same size and in the same location. One image is upright, and the other is inverted. Both images are 1.50 times larger than the object. The lens has a focal length of \(10.0\text{ cm}\). The lens and mirror are separated by \(40.0\text{ cm}\). Determine the focal length of the mirror.

A train car moves with a constant velocity to the right along the ground. A light signal is emitted from the left end $A$ of the train car, reflects off a mirror at the right end $B$, and returns to end $A$ as shown in the figure. As seen by an observer on the ground, how long does it take for the light signal to reach end $B$?
An observer measures the length of a 10 m rod to be 8 m when it is in motion as shown in figure. What is the speed of the rod relative to the observer?

Assume a transparent rod of diameter \( d = 2.00 \, \mu\text{m} \) has an index of refraction of 1.36. Determine the maximum angle \( \theta \) for which the light rays incident on the end of the rod in the figure are subject to total internal reflection along the walls of the rod.

An airplane in flight is subject to an air resistance force proportional to the square of its speed \( v \). But there is an additional resistive force because the airplane has wings. Air flowing over the wings is pushed down and slightly forward, so from Newton's third law the air exerts a force on the wings and airplane that is up and slightly backward as shown in the figure. The upward force is the lift force that keeps the airplane aloft, and the backward force is called induced drag. At...

As shown in Figure, a horizontal light ray passes through a prism with a refractive index of 1.50. After exiting the prism, it is reflected by a vertically placed reflecting mirror. How many degrees should the reflecting mirror be rotated clockwise so that the reflected light ray is horizontal?

A train car is moving relative to the ground as shown in figure. A ball moves relative to the car, in the same direction as the car's motion. How long does it take for the ball to go from the rear end to the front end of the car, as observed by a ground observer?

A system of two paint buckets connected by a lightweight rope is released from rest, as shown in the figure. Find the speed with which this bucket strikes the floor. Ignore friction and the mass of the pulley.

A snow-covered ski slope makes an angle with the horizontal. When a ski jumper plummets onto the hill, a parcel of splashed snow is thrown up to a maximum displacement of \(1.50\ \text{m}\) in the uphill direction as shown in the figure. Find the component of its maximum displacement perpendicular to the surface.

In the Young's experiment setup shown in the figure, if the wavelength of the monochromatic light is λ = 500.0 nm, d = S₁S₂ = 0.33 cm, r₀ = 3 m. If a parallel plane glass plate with a thickness h = 0.01 mm is placed behind slit S₂, and the fringe displacement is 4.73 mm, calculate the refractive index of the glass.

As shown in the figure, a conical tube with openings at both ends has apex angle $2\delta$. Light is reflected between its inner walls. A ray enters from the larger opening. After $m$ reflections inside the tube, what is the incident angle?
In the circuit shown in Figure, the switch is initially at A. If the switch is suddenly turned to B, what is the maximum voltage $V_{\text{max}}$ that appears across the capacitor?

In the laboratory, the measured velocity of an electron is as shown in figure. Suppose an observer moves in the same direction as the electron relative to the laboratory. Find the kinetic energy as measured by this observer. (The rest mass of the electron is $m_e = 9.11 \times 10^{-31} \text{ kg}$)

final_category_zh: 近代物理、量子与相对论
question: A radionuclide with half-life $T = 14.3\ \text{days}$ is produced in a reactor at a constant rate $q = 10^9$ nuclei per second, as shown in the figure. How soon after the beginning of production of that radionuclide will its activity be equal to $A = 10^8$ disintegrations per second?
A particle is in a one-dimensional box with a length of $L$. If the particle is in the eigenstate with the energy eigenvalue $E_n$, what is the force exerted by the particle on the wall of the box?

Block \(A\) in figure has mass 4.00 kg, and block \(B\) has mass 12.0 kg. The coefficient of kinetic friction between block \(B\) and the horizontal surface is 0.25. What is the mass of block \(C\) if block \(B\) is moving to the right and speeding up with an acceleration of \(2.00\,\text{m/s}^2\)?

Police radar detects the speed of a car as shown in figure. Microwaves of a precisely known frequency are broadcast toward the car. The moving car reflects the microwaves with a Doppler shift. The reflected waves are received and combined with an attenuated version of the transmitted wave. Beats occur between the two microwave signals. The beat frequency is measured. What beat frequency is measured for a car speed of \(30.0\,\text{m/s}\) if the microwaves have frequency \(10.0\,\text{GHz}\)?
A plane simple harmonic wave has its waveform at $t=0$ shown in the figure, and the wave speed $u=0.08\ \text{m/s}$. Write the equation for this wave.

As shown in Figure, a light ray parallel to the optical axis and close to the axis is incident on a concave mirror. For the case where $y \ll R$ (paraxial approximation), find the focal position $X_0 = X_0(R)$.

A spaceship traveling at a velocity. When the tail of the spaceship passes a certain signal station on the ground, the signal station emits a light signal as shown in figure. What is the time, as measured by an observer on the ground, that it takes for the signal to reach the head of the spaceship, $\Delta t$?

Given that $q$ and $l$ in Figure are both known, such a configuration of four point charges is called a planar quadrupole. In the figure, point A and the center of the quadrupole lie in the same plane, the distance from point A to the center of the quadrupole O is x, and AO is parallel to the two sides of the square. Find the electric field strength $\mathbf{E}$ at point A.

Taking into account the motion of the nucleus of a hydrogen atom, find the expressions for the electron's binding energy in the ground state as shown in the figure. How much (in per cent) does the binding energy obtained without taking into account the motion of the nucleus differ from the more accurate corresponding value of this quantity? Given $\frac{m}{M} = 0.00055$, where $m$ and $M$ are the masses of an electron and a proton.

As shown in Figure, in a Young's interference experiment setup (the single slit in front is not drawn), a thin convex lens $L$ with a focal length of 10 cm is placed at the right of the double-slit screen $S_1S_2$ (the axis of symmetry of the interference device). A screen is placed at the right of $L$, perpendicular to the optical axis. Given the double-slit separation $d = 0.2 \text{mm}$, and using light with a wavelength of $\lambda = 500 \text{nm}$ for illumination, calculate the fringe s...
As shown in Figure, a guitar string is pulled up from the middle of its length. Calculate the amplitudes of the third harmonics, A3 (relative to the amplitude of the fundamental mode A0).
A student drives a moped along a straight road as described by the velocity--time graph in figure. What is the moped's final position at \( t = 9.00 \text{ s} \)?

Two converging lenses having focal lengths of \( f_1 = 10.0 \text{ cm} \) and \( f_2 = 20.0 \text{ cm} \) are placed a distance \( d = 50.0 \text{ cm} \) apart as shown in the figure. The image due to light passing through both lenses is to be located between the lenses at the position \( x = 31.0 \text{ cm} \) indicated. At what value of \( p \) should the object be positioned to the left of the first lens?
A particle starts from rest and accelerates as shown in the figure. Determine the distance traveled in the first \(20.0\ \text{s}\).

Two sound sources S1 and S2 are separated, as shown in the figure. There are two observers at points A and B, which are on the same line as the sound sources. S1 emits a stable plane wave, whose excess pressure is given by $p_e = A \cos(200t)$; the excess pressure of the stable plane wave emitted by S2 is given by $p_e = A \cos(210t)$. The speed of sound is taken as 340 m s⁻¹. What is the time interval $\Delta t$ between the maximum pulses heard by A and B?

A rectangular parallelepiped as shown in figure. Notice that \( \vec{R}_1 \), \( c\hat{k} \), and \( \vec{R}_2 \) make a right triangle. Obtain a vector expression for the body diagonal vector \( \vec{R}_2 \).

Two parallel rulers have a length of $l_0$ in their respective rest frames as shown in the figure. They move with the same velocity $v$ relative to a certain inertial frame, but in opposite directions and parallel to the rulers. What is the length of the other ruler as measured on one ruler?

Suppose a spaceship is launched from Earth, travels in a straight line to the Moon as shown in the figure. The speed of the spaceship is measured on Earth. How long does the trip take according to the measurements on the spaceship?

A radio telescope antenna is set above the lake surface. A star is just rising above the horizon on the opposite shore. The star emits electromagnetic waves with wavelength $\lambda$. Find the angular position $\theta$ of the star when the antenna detects the first interference maximum.

A basketball (which can be closely modeled as a hollow spherical shell) rolls down a mountainside into a valley and then up the opposite side, starting from rest above the bottom. In the figure, the rough part of the terrain prevents slipping while the smooth part has no friction. Neglect rolling friction and assume the system’s total mechanical energy is conserved. How high, in terms of \(H_0\), will the ball go up the other side?

An object AB with a length of 4 cm forms an image through the optical system shown in the figure. The optical system consists of a right-angled prism with a refractive index of 1.5, a converging thin lens with a focal length $f'_1 = 20\,\text{cm}$, and a diverging thin lens with a focal length $f'_2 = -10\,\text{cm}$. Other geometric dimensions are marked in the figure. Determine the size of the image.

A grindstone is a solid disk \(0.520\text{ m}\) in diameter. You press an ax down on the rim with a normal force as shown in the figure. The coefficient of kinetic friction between the blade and the stone is \(0.60\), and there is a constant friction torque of \(6.50\text{ N}\cdot\text{m}\) between the axle of the stone and its bearings. How much time does it take the grindstone to come from \(120\text{ rev/min}\) to rest if it is acted on by the axle friction alone?
Under the double-slit condition shown in the figure, find where the first minimum appears.

A cylinder containing helium gas is equipped with a piston of mass m and area A, pushed by a spring. At equilibrium, the total pressure of the helium gas, and the piston of the system are as shown in Figure. The spring (with spring constant K) is compressed by $x_0$ (its free length is $L_0 + x_0$). If the gas undergoes isothermal compression, find the angular frequency $\omega$ of small oscillations ($x \ll L_0$) of the piston.

Two pipes are separated by a distance d, entering perpendicularly and passing through a large wall of thickness t, as shown in the figure. The thermal conductivity of the wall is K, and the temperature far from the pipes is T0. At x = +d/2, hot water flows in the pipe, transferring heat to the wall at a rate of W watts. At x = -d/2, cold water flows in the pipe, absorbing heat from the wall at a rate of W watts. Consider this as a two-dimensional problem, neglecting the finite size effects of...

High-frequency signals are often transmitted along a coaxial cable, such as the one shown in figure. For example, the cable TV hookup coming into your home is a coaxial cable. The signal is carried on a wire while the outer conductor is grounded. A soft, flexible insulating material fills the space between them, and an insulating plastic coating goes around the outside. Evaluate the inductance per meter of a cable having \( r_1 = 0.50 \, \text{mm} \) and \( r_2 = 3.0 \, \text{mm} \).

A cylindrical plano-concave lens A with radius of curvature R is placed on a flat glass plate B, as shown in the figure. Monochromatic parallel light with wavelength λ is incident perpendicularly from above to observe the interference fringes of reflected light from the air film between A and B. Assume the maximum thickness of the air film is d = 2λ. How many bright fringes can be observed in total?

A bowling ball of mass \( M \) rolls without slipping down a ramp as shown in the figure. What is the magnitude of the friction force on the ball? Treat the ball as a uniform solid sphere, ignoring the finger holes.

Assume that the de-Broglie wave associated with an electron can form a standing wave between the atoms arranged in a one dimensional array with nodes at each of the atomic sites. It is found that one such standing wave is formed if the distance $d$ between the atoms of the array is 2 Å. A similar standing wave is again formed if $d$ is increased to 2.5 Å but not for any intermediate value of $d$ as shown in figure. Find the energy of the electron in eV and the least value of $d$ for which the...

Suppose that two cosmic-ray protons approach Earth from opposite directions as shown in figure. The speeds relative to Earth are measured to be \( v_1 = 0.6c \) and \( v_2 = -0.8c \). What is the velocity of each proton relative to the other?

A 350 kg roller coaster car starts from rest at point A and slides down a frictionless loop-the-loop as shown in the figure. The car's wheels are designed to stay on the track. How hard does it press against the track at point B?

As shown in the figure, half of the container is filled with nitrogen gas, and the other half is evacuated. The two parts of the container are separated by a piston, and the piston has a small valve through it. Open the small valve; the gas flows from one end of the container to the other until equilibrium is reached. Slowly move the piston to one end of the container. Assume the initial temperature of the helium gas is $T_0$ and the pressure is $P_0$. Quantitatively compare the final state $...

Figure shows a single-gap spark chamber. The gap between the two aluminum plates of the spark chamber is $d$. A charged cosmic ray particle ionizes neon molecules along its path. After time $t$, a high-voltage pulse is applied across the two aluminum plates, and sparks are observed along the particle's path. In order to eliminate residual electrons and ions in the spark chamber from cosmic rays that entered previously, a DC voltage is applied across the two aluminum plates. This "clearing vol...

The yoga exercise "Downward-Facing Dog" requires stretching your hands straight out above your head and bending down to lean against the floor. This exercise is performed by a \(750\,\text{N}\) person as shown in the figure. When he bends his body at the hip, his legs, trunk, head, and arms have the dimensions indicated. Furthermore, his legs and feet weigh a total of \(277\,\text{N}\), and their center of mass is \(41\,\text{cm}\) from his hip, measured along his legs. The person’s trunk, he...
In the circuit shown in Figure, the driving frequency is $\omega = 1/\sqrt{LC}$. Find the maximum current $I_{L, \text{max}}$ through $L$.

A thin, uniform, \(3.80\text{ kg}\) bar, \(80.0\text{ cm}\) long, has very small balls glued on at either end, as shown in the figure. It is supported horizontally by a thin, horizontal, frictionless axle passing through its center and perpendicular to the bar. Suddenly the right-hand ball becomes detached and falls off, but the other ball remains glued to the bar. Find the angular velocity of the bar just as it swings through its vertical position.
Figure shows a hollow cylinder of uniform mass density \( \rho \). (It might be a steel cylinder in a printing press.) Find its moment of inertia about its axis of symmetry

A 15.0 kg stone slides down a snow-covered hill as shown in figure, leaving point A at a speed of 10.0 m/s. There is no friction on the hill between points A and B. After entering the rough horizontal region, the stone travels 100 m and then runs into a very long, light spring with force constant 2.00 N/m. The coefficients of kinetic and static friction between the stone and the horizontal ground are 0.20 and 0.80, respectively. How far will the stone compress the spring?

A small shot of negligible radius hits a stationary smooth, hard sphere of radius \( R \), making an angle \( \beta \) with the normal to the sphere, as shown in the figure. It is reflected at an equal angle to the normal. The scattering angle is \( \theta = 180^{\circ} - 2\beta \), as shown. If the incoming intensity of the shot is \( I_0 \) particles/s \( \cdot \) area, how many are scattered through angles greater than \( \theta \)?

A cylindrical glass beaker contains a metal cylindrical rod as shown in the figure. When observed from the outside of the beaker, the diameter of the rod is $D_1$ when there is no water in the beaker, and the diameter is $D_2$ when the beaker is filled with water. Determine the refractive index $n$ of water.

A thief hides a precious jewel by placing it on the bottom of a public swimming pool. He places a circular raft on the surface of the water directly above and centered over the jewel, as shown in the figure. The surface of the water is calm. The raft, of diameter \(d = 4.54\ \text{m}\), prevents the jewel from being seen by any observer above the water, either on the raft or on the side of the pool. What is the maximum depth \(h\) of the pool for the jewel to remain unseen?

final_category_zh: 近代物理、量子与相对论
question: Figure shows a pulse train. What is the maximum pulse-transmission rate (pulses per second) through an electronics system with a \(200\ \text{kHz}\) bandwidth? (This is the bandwidth allotted to each FM radio station.)

Two thin rods are fastened to the inside of a circular ring as shown in the figure. One rod is vertical, and the other makes an angle with the horizontal. The two rods and the ring lie in a vertical plane. Two small beads are free to slide without friction along the rods. Find an expression for the time interval required for the blue bead to slide from point \textcircled{B} to point \textcircled{C} in terms of \( g \), \( L \), and \( \theta \).

A thin, light wire is wrapped around the rim of a wheel as shown in the figure. The wheel rotates without friction about a stationary horizontal axis that passes through the center of the wheel. The wheel is a uniform disk with radius \(R = 0.280\text{ m}\). An object of mass \(m = 4.20\text{ kg}\) is suspended from the free end of the wire. The system is released from rest, and the suspended object descends with constant acceleration. If the suspended object moves downward a distance of 3.00...

A narrow beam of ultrasonic waves reflects off the liver tumor illustrated in figure. The speed of the wave is \( 10.0\% \) less in the liver than in the surrounding medium. Determine the depth of the tumor.
The cutoff frequency of potassium is $0.462 \times 10^{15} \text{ Hz}$. Now it is irradiated with light as shown in figure. The threshold wavelength of potassium.
Figure shows a 10-cm-diameter loop in magnetic fields. The loop's resistance is \(0.20\,\Omega\). What is the size of the induced current?

A small circular loop (1) carries a steady current $I$. Another circular loop (2) is coaxial with (1). The distance between the two loops is $\gg a$. The planes of the two loops are parallel. As shown in the figure, loop (2) now rotates about one of its diameters with angular velocity $\omega$. Loop (2) is broken (open circuit), so no current flows through it. Find the induced electromotive force $\epsilon$.
As shown in the figure, a ray of light enters a spherical water droplet of refractive index $n$. What is the incident angle $i$ when the deviation angle $\delta$ is at its minimum?

An outfielder throws a baseball to his catcher in an attempt to throw out a runner at home plate. The ball bounces once before reaching the catcher as shown in figure, but that the ball's speed after the bounce is one-half of what it was before the bounce. Determine the ratio of the time interval for the one-bounce throw to the flight time for the no-bounce throw.

As shown in the figure, a thin lens ($r_1=-r_2=20\,\text{cm}$) is placed in air. A real image of point A is formed at point B. Find the refractive index $n$ of the thin lens. If a negative thin lens with focal length $f'=-180\,\text{mm}$ is then placed in contact with the original lens, determine the position of the final image.
A full-wave rectifier is an instrument that can convert a sine wave with amplitude V0 into the waveform shown in Figure. Find the amplitude A2 of the second harmonic component of the output voltage.

The circuit shown in Figure forms a relaxation oscillator. It is composed of a neon bulb and a capacitor. The capacitor is charged by a DC power source through a resistor. When the voltage across the neon bulb is less than 60 V, its resistance is infinite. When the voltage reaches or exceeds 60 V, the neon bulb breaks down (ignites), its resistance becomes very small, causing the capacitor to discharge. The neon bulb quickly returns to infinite resistance. Find the flashing frequency f of the...
What value of resistor \( R \) gives the circuit in the figure a time constant of \( 25 \, \mu\text{s} \)?

A certain radar emits $n$ electromagnetic pulses per second. The pulses are perpendicularly emitted to a spacecraft moving towards the radar as shown in figure. Considering relativistic effects, how many reflected pulses does the radar receive per second?

A cylindrical buoy with a uniform cross-section floats vertically in water when there are no waves in the ocean. If the undisturbed submerged length is 100 ft, the wave height is 10 ft, and the wave period is 5 s, find the amplitude of the buoy's motion.

A very long conducting rod has a hole inside it. The axis of the hole is parallel to the axis of the rod and separated by a distance $d$, as shown in Figure. A uniform current density $j$ flows through the conductor. What is the magnitude of the magnetic field $\boldsymbol{B}$ at a location on the axis of the hole far from the ends?
A ring with mass $m$ is placed on a knife edge. The ring can oscillate in its own plane, forming a physical pendulum, as shown in the figure. Find the period $T$ for small oscillations of this ring.

The lens and mirror in the figure are separated and have focal lengths of \(+80.0\text{ cm}\) and \(-50.0\text{ cm}\), respectively. An object is placed to the left of the lens as shown. Determine the overall magnification of the image.

Consider an infinite square well potential with walls at \(x = 0\) and \(x = L\); that is, \(V(x) = 0\) for \(0 < x < L\) and \(V(x) = \infty\) otherwise. Now impose a perturbation on this potential of the form \(H' = L V_0 \delta(x - L/2)\), where \(\delta(x)\) is the Dirac delta function. Now consider the case where we impose a perturbation on the infinite square well potential as shown in the figure, with \(\varepsilon\) a small number. Calculate the first-order correction to the energy of...

As shown in Figure, an electron initially at rest at $x = 0, z = 0$ is subjected to an acceleration $\boldsymbol{a}$ for $t > 0$. $\boldsymbol{a}$ is in the $xz$-plane and makes an angle $\theta$ with the $z$-axis. Calculate (relativistically) the electric field $\boldsymbol{E}(t)$ at the point $(0, 0, R_0)$, where $R_0$ is a 'large' distance. Express the result in terms of $\boldsymbol{a}$, $\theta$, and $\beta = v/c$, where $v$ is the electron's speed.

Two large metal plates are placed parallel to each other. One end of the two plates is connected by a metallic strip. A thin plastic sheet with a surface charge density $\sigma$ is placed between the two plates as shown in the figure. Calculate the electric field $\vec{E}_1$ near the upper plate.
The curve shown in the figure was obtained in a photoelectric effect experiment. Determine Planck's constant \(h\) from the data in the figure.

Block \( A \), with weight \( 3w \), slides down an inclined plane \( S \) at a constant speed while plank \( B \), with weight \( w \), rests on top of \( A \). The plank is attached by a cord to the wall as shown in the figure. If the coefficient of kinetic friction is the same between \( A \) and \( B \) and between \( S \) and \( A \), determine its value.
The magnitudes of the vectors are \(A = 4.00\) and \(B = 5.00\). Find the scalar product \(\vec{A} \cdot \vec{B}\) of the two vectors in the figure.

A light ray enters a rectangular block of plastic at an angle \(\theta_1 = 45.0^{\circ}\) and emerges at an angle \(\theta_2 = 76.0^{\circ}\) as shown in the figure. If the light ray enters the plastic at a point \(L = 50.0\ \text{cm}\) from the bottom edge, what time interval is required for the light ray to travel through the plastic?

A movie stuntman stands on a window ledge above the floor as shown in the figure. Grabbing a rope attached to a chandelier, he swings down to grapple with the movie's villain, who is standing directly under the chandelier. Assume that the stuntman's center of mass moves downward 5.0 m. He releases the rope just as he reaches the villain. If the coefficient of kinetic friction of their bodies with the floor is \(\mu_k = 0.250\), how far do they slide?

The circuit shown in the figure has the following characteristics: $V(t)=V_0\cos\omega t$, $V_0=10\text{ V}$, $\omega=25\times10^3\text{ rad s}^{-1}$, $L=4\times10^{-3}\text{ H}$, $C=4\times10^{-7}\text{ F}$, $R=160\,\Omega$. Consider the circuit in the steady-state oscillation. Find the amplitude of the current $I_0$.

As shown in Figure 3.8, a prism with a small apex angle α (the so-called "light wedge") has a refractive index n. A plane light wave with wavelength λ is incident normally from one side. Assuming the initial phase of the incident wave at the left surface Σ₀ of the light wedge is 0, find the wavefront phase function on the plane Σ₁ at a distance s from Σ₀ on the right side of the light wedge.

A transparent cylinder of radius \(R = 2.00\ \text{m}\) is shown in the figure. A light ray traveling in air is incident on the left side of the cylinder. The incident light ray and exiting light ray are parallel, and \(d = 2.00\ \text{m}\). Determine the index of refraction of the material.

A large-headed pin is vertically inserted into the center of a circular wooden plug. Then, it is placed upside down on the water surface, and the length of the large-headed pin exposed is adjusted. The observer cannot see the large-headed pin under the water surface from any angle on the water surface, as shown in Figure. If the length of the large-headed pin exposed outside the wooden plug is measured as $h$, and the diameter of the wooden plug is $d$, find the refractive index of water.

In laminar flow, the viscosity of the liquid completely determines the rate of liquid transmission in the pipe between two reservoirs with different pressures ($P_1 > P_2$). In Figure, the liquid flows through a cylindrical pipe with length $L$ ($L$ is much greater than the mean free path $l$). The viscosity coefficient of the liquid is $\eta$. Find that for steady state, the liquid flow rate per second from reservoir 1 to reservoir 2

A pulley on a frictionless axle has the shape of a uniform solid disk and radius 20.0 cm. A stone is attached to a very light wire that is wrapped around the rim of the pulley, as shown in the figure, and the system is released from rest. How far must the stone fall so that the pulley has 4.50 J of kinetic energy?
The vibration curve of a vibrating particle is shown in the figure. Find the time required to reach the position corresponding to point P from the start of the vibration.

Two metal disks, one with radius \(R_1 = 2.50\text{ cm}\) and mass \(M_1 = 0.80\text{ kg}\) and the other with radius \(R_2 = 5.00\text{ cm}\) and mass \(M_2 = 1.60\text{ kg}\), are welded together and mounted on a frictionless axis through their common center as shown in the figure. A light string is wrapped around the edge of the smaller disk, and a \(1.50\text{ kg}\) block is suspended from the free end of the string. If the block is released from rest at a distance of \(2.00\text{ m}\) ab...
