An 8.00 kg box sits on a ramp. The coefficient of kinetic friction between the box and the surface of the ramp is \(\mu_{\text{k}} = 0.300\). A constant horizontal force \(F = 26.0\text{ N}\) is applied to the box as shown in the figure, and the box moves down the ramp. If the box is initially at rest, what is its speed 2.00 s after the force is applied?

Towns A and B in figure are apart. A couple arranges to drive from town A and meet a couple driving from town B at the lake, L. The two couples leave simultaneously and drive for \(2.50\ \text{h}\) in the directions shown. Car 1 has a speed of \(90.0\ \text{km/h}\). If the cars arrive simultaneously at the lake, what is the speed of car 2?

A lens is formed by combining a spherical glass surface with a radius of curvature of $20\,\text{cm}$ and a plane glass surface, creating an air lens. The lens is then submerged in water (see Figure). Assuming the thickness of the glass walls is negligible, the refractive indices of water and air are $4/3$ and $1$, respectively. Determine the focal length of this lens.

An early method testing Maxwell's theoretical prediction for the distribution of molecular speeds is shown in the figure. In 1925 Otto Stern used a beam of \(\text{Bi}_2\) molecules emitted from an oven at 850 K. The beam defined by slit \(\text{S}_1\) was admitted into the interior of a rotating drum via slit \(\text{S}_2\) in the drum wall. The identical bunches of molecules thus formed struck and adhered to a curved glass plate fixed to the interior drum wall, the fastest molecules strikin...

Particle A has a rest energy of \(1192\ \text{MeV}\) and is moving through the laboratory in the positive \(x\) direction. It decays into particle B (rest energy = \(1116\ \text{MeV}\)) and a photon; particle A disappears in the decay process. The photon moves in a direction at an angle \(\theta\) with the positive \(x\) axis as shown in the figure. Find the angle \(\theta\).

An atom of uranium (\( m = 3.9529 \times 10^{-25} \) kg) at rest decays spontaneously into an atom of helium (\( m = 6.6465 \times 10^{-27} \) kg) and an atom of thorium (\( m = 3.8864 \times 10^{-25} \) kg). The helium atom is observed to move in the positive \( x \) direction with a velocity of \( 1.423 \times 10^7 \) m/s as shown in figure. Find the total kinetic energy of the two atoms after the decay.

Sir Lancelot rides slowly out of the castle at Camelot and onto the drawbridge that passes over the moat. Unbeknownst to him, his enemies have partially severed the vertical cable holding up the front end of the bridge so that it will break under a tension of \( 5.80 \times 10^3\, \text{N} \). The bridge has mass \( 200\, \text{kg} \) and its center of gravity is at its center. Lancelot, his lance, his armor, and his horse together have a combined mass of \( 600\, \text{kg} \). How far from t...

The figure shows the probability density for an electron that has passed through an experimental apparatus. What is the probability that the electron will land in a \(0.010\text{-mm}\)-wide strip at \(x = 0.000\text{ mm}\)?

A periscope as shown in figure is useful for viewing objects that cannot be seen directly. It can be used in submarines and when watching golf matches or parades from behind a crowd of people. Suppose the object is a distance from the upper mirror and the centers of the two flat mirrors are separated. What is the distance of the final image from the lower mirror?
In a repair shop, a truck engine that has mass 409 kg is held in place by four light cables as shown in the figure. If the tension in cable A is 722 N, what are the tensions in cable B?

A train of length $l_0$ moves at a constant velocity $v$ to the right as shown in figure. When it passes a platform of the same length, an observer on the platform finds that the left end of the platform and the tail of the train coincide. After a time interval of $\Delta t$, the right end of the platform and the head of the train coincide. Find the velocity $v$ of the train.

Two blocks are connected by a string and slide down an inclined plane as shown in the figure. The coefficient of kinetic friction between the 4.00 kg block and the plane is 0.25; that between the 8.00 kg block and the plane is 0.35. What is the tension in the string?

In the figure, a thin converging lens of focal length 14.0 cm forms an image of the square \(abcd\), which is \(h_c = h_b = 10.0\ \text{cm}\) high and lies between distances of \(p_d = 20.0\ \text{cm}\) and \(p_a = 30.0\ \text{cm}\) from the lens. Let \(a'\), \(b'\), \(c'\), and \(d'\) represent the respective corners of the image. Let \(q_a\) represent the image distance for points \(a'\) and \(b'\), \(q_d\) represent the image distance for points \(c'\) and \(d'\), \(h_b'\) represent the di...

As shown in Figure, a thick lens with a refractive index of 1.5 has a radius of curvature of 3 cm for its lower surface. The central thickness of the lens is 2 cm, and it is placed on water. The refractive index of water is 4/3. An object with a height of 2 mm is placed below the thick lens on the optical axis in the water, and the distance between the object and the center of the lower surface of the thick lens is 4 cm. Find the height of the final image under the paraxial approximation.

In a plastic film factory, a thin plastic wide belt moves at a speed $v$ between two adjacent rollers, as shown in Figure. During the manufacturing process, the thin film accumulates a uniform surface charge $\sigma$. Find the magnetic field $\mathbf{B}$ in the same region.

An experiment was performed in which neutrons were shot through two slits spaced \(0.10\text{ nm}\) apart and detected \(3.5\text{ m}\) behind the slits. Figure shows the detector output. To one significant figure, what was the speed of the neutrons?
The position versus time for a certain particle moving along the \(x\) axis is shown in the figure. Find the average velocity in the time interval from \(0\) to \(8\,\text{s}\).

Block A in the figure weighs 1.20 N, and block B weighs 3.60 N. The coefficient of kinetic friction between all surfaces is 0.300. Find the magnitude of the horizontal force \(\vec{F}\) necessary to drag block B to the left at constant speed if A is held at rest, as shown in the figure.

As shown in Figure, a charge $+q$ is uniformly distributed on a thin circular ring. The ring is located in the $yz$ plane with its center at the origin, and a charge $-q$ is placed at the center of the ring. Find the potential $\phi$ at point $P=(x, 0, 0)$.

As shown in Figure, two bulbs with volumes of 200 cm3 and 100 cm3 respectively are connected by a short tube fitted with an insulating porous plug. The porous plug allows the pressure in the two bulbs to become equal, while the temperatures can be different. The system is sealed when it contains oxygen gas at a temperature of 27°C and a pressure of 760 mmHg. The small bulb is immersed in an ice bath at 0°C and the large bulb is in a steam bath at 100°C. What is the final pressure $P$ of the s...

A 2.0 kg piece of wood slides on a curved surface as shown in the figure. The sides of the surface are perfectly smooth, but the rough horizontal bottom is 30 m long and has a kinetic friction coefficient of 0.20 with the wood. The piece of wood starts from rest 4.0 m above the rough bottom. For the motion from the initial release until the piece of wood comes to rest, what is the total amount of work done by friction?

Van der Waals bonds arise from the interaction between two permanent or induced electric dipole moments in a pair of atoms or molecules. Consider two identical dipoles, separated by a distance \(d\) and oriented as shown in the figure. Calculate the electric potential energy, expressed in terms of the electric dipole moment \(p = qd\), for the situation where \(r \gg d\).

A digital video disc (DVD) records information in a spiral track approximately \(1\ \mu\text{m}\) wide. The track consists of a series of pits in the information layer that scatter light from a laser beam sharply focused on them. The laser shines in from below through transparent plastic of thickness \(t = 1.20\ \text{mm}\) as shown in the figure. Assume the width of the laser beam at the information layer must be \(a = 1.00\ \mu\text{m}\) to read from only one track and not from its neighbor...
Figure represents the total acceleration of a particle moving clockwise in a circle at a certain instant of time. For that instant, find its tangential acceleration.
In terms of \(m_1\), \(m_2\), and \(g\), find the acceleration of each block in the figure. There is no friction anywhere in the system.
For the circuit shown in the figure, what is the resonance frequency, in \( \text{Hz} \)?
The switch in figure has been in position 1 for a long time. It is changed to position 2 at \( t = 0 \, \text{s} \). What is the first time at which the current is maximum?

As shown in the figure, the radii of curvature of the two spherical surfaces of a thin concave lens are equal. If the refractive indices of the object and image sides are 1.0 and 2.0 respectively, what should be the refractive index of this lens so that the object can finally form an upright image of the same size under the paraxial condition?

A beam of spin-\(1/2\) particles is sent through a series of three Stern-Gerlach analyzers, as shown in the figure. The second Stern-Gerlach analyzer is aligned along the \(\hat{n}\) direction, which makes an angle \(\theta\) in the \(x\)-\(z\) plane with respect to the \(z\)-axis. Find the probability that particles transmitted through the first Stern-Gerlach analyzer are measured to have spin down at the third Stern-Gerlach analyzer.

Figure shows a thin converging lens for which the radii of curvature of its surfaces have magnitudes of \( 9.00 \text{ cm} \) and \( 11.0 \text{ cm} \). The lens is in front of a con- cave spherical mirror with the radius of curvature \( R = 8.00 \text{ cm} \). Assume the focal points \( F_1 \) and \( F_2 \) of the lens are \( 5.00 \text{ cm} \) from the center of the lens. Determine the index of refraction of the lens material. The lens and mirror are \( 20.0 \text{ cm} \) apart, and an obje...
The Compton scattered photons are measured at a scattering angle as shown in the figure. If $\frac{\Delta \lambda}{\lambda_0} = 1\%$, then what is the wavelength $\lambda_0$ of the incident photons?

An object is at \( x = 0 \) at \( t = 0 \) and moves along the \( x \) axis according to the velocity-time graph in the figure. Through what total distance has the object moved between \( t = 0 \) and \( t = 18.0\ \text{s} \)?

A \(72.0\,\text{kg}\) weightlifter doing arm raises holds a weight. Her arm pivots around the elbow joint, starting below the horizontal. Biometric measurements have shown that, together, the forearms and the hands account for \(6.00\%\) of a person’s weight. Since the upper arm is held vertically, the biceps muscle always acts vertically and is attached to the bones of the forearm \(5.50\,\text{cm}\) from the elbow joint. The center of mass of this person’s forearm–hand combination is \(16.0...

An elevator and its load have a combined mass of \( 800 \mathrm{kg} \) as shown in figure. The elevator is initially moving downward at \( 10.0 \mathrm{m/s} \); it slows to a stop with constant acceleration in a distance of \( 25.0 \mathrm{m} \). What is the tension \( T \) in the supporting cable while the elevator is being brought to rest?
A horizontal wire holds a solid uniform ball of mass \( m \) in place on a tilted ramp. The surface of this ramp is perfectly smooth as shown in figure. What is the tension in the wire?

A primitive yo-yo has a massless string wrapped around a solid cylinder as shown in the figure. You hold the free end of the string stationary and release the cylinder from rest. The string unwinds but does not slip or stretch as the cylinder descends and rotates. Find the speed \( v_{cm} \) of the cylinder's center of mass after it has descended a distance \( h \).

Figure shows an elastic collision of two pucks on a frictionless air-hockey table. Puck A has an initial velocity in the positive \( x \)-direction and a final velocity in an unknown direction \( \alpha \). Puck B is initially at rest. Find the final speed \( v_{\text{B2}} \) of puck B.

Two forces equal in magnitude and opposite in direction form what is called a \textit{couple}. Two antiparallel forces with equal magnitudes \(F_1 = F_2 = 8.00\,\text{N}\) are applied to a rod as shown in the figure. What should the distance \(l\) between the forces be if they are to provide a net torque of \(6.40\,\text{N}\cdot\text{m}\) about the left end of the rod?

The figure illustrates an Atwood's machine. If there is no slipping between the cord and the surface of the wheel, let the masses of blocks \(A\) and \(B\) be \(4.00\text{ kg}\) and \(2.00\text{ kg}\), respectively, the moment of inertia of the wheel about its axis be \(0.220\text{ kg}\cdot\text{m}^2\), and the radius of the wheel be \(0.120\text{ m}\). Find the angular acceleration of the wheel \(C\).

As shown in Figure, the diagram shows a Young's double-slit interference experiment, where a monochromatic parallel light with wavelength $\lambda$ is incident perpendicularly onto double slits with a slit width of $a$ ($a \ll \lambda$). Therefore, interference fringes are observed on the screen at a distance. A thin glass sheet with thickness $d$ and refractive index $n$ is placed between the slits and the screen. If the thickness $d$ is variable, then at the center point O on the screen, wh...
An airplane's compass indicates that it is headed due north, as shown in the figure. If there is a wind from west to east, what is the velocity of the airplane relative to the earth?
The emf phasor is shown in the figure. What is the instantaneous value of the emf?

A photon of frequency \(
u\) undergoes elastic scattering from a stationary free electron, as shown in the figure. The energy of the photon is \(h
u\). Considering the photon-electron system, use momentum conservation, energy conservation, and the relativistic energy-momentum relation to derive the relationship between \(
u'\) and \(
u\).

A truck with mass \(m\) has a brake failure while going down an icy mountain road of constant downward slope as shown in the figure. Initially the truck is moving downhill at speed \(v_0\). After careening downhill a distance \(L\) with negligible friction, the truck driver steers the runaway vehicle onto a runaway truck ramp of constant upward slope. The truck ramp has a soft sand surface for which the coefficient of rolling friction is \(\mu_r\). What is the distance that the truck moves up...

The speed of the Earth in its orbit is \(29.8 \, \text{km/s}\). If that is the magnitude of the velocity \(\vec{v}\) of the ether wind in the figure, find the angle \(\phi\) between the velocity of light \(\vec{c}\) in vacuum and the resultant velocity of light if there were an ether.
Figure shows a square bent at a \(90^\circ\) angle. A uniform \(0.050\,\text{T}\) magnetic field points downward at a angle. What is the magnetic flux through the loop?

A landscape architect is planning an artificial waterfall in a city park. Water flowing at \(1.70\ \text{m/s}\) will leave the end of a horizontal channel at the top of a vertical wall \(h = 2.35\ \text{m}\) high, and from there it will fall into a pool as shown in the figure. To sell her plan to the city council, the architect wants to build a model to standard scale, which is one-twelfth actual size. How fast should the water flow in the channel in the model?

Two identical masses are released from rest in a smooth bowl from the positions shown in the figure. Ignore friction between the masses and the surface of the bowl. If the masses stick together when they collide, how high above the bottom of the bowl will they go after colliding?

A parallel beam of light enters a glass hemisphere perpendicular to the flat face as shown in Figure P36.84. The magnitude of the radius of the hemisphere is \( R = 6.00 \text{ cm} \), and its index of refraction is \( n = 1.560 \). Assuming paraxial rays, determine the point at which the beam is focused.

A plane harmonic wave propagates along the positive x-axis. At $t=0$, the oscillation of the particle at the origin O is moving toward the negative direction after passing the equilibrium position. There is a reflection surface from a denser medium at a distance $x_0=\frac{11}{4}\lambda$ from the origin O, and the wave is reflected perpendicularly by the interface. Assume the amplitudes of both the incident wave and the reflected wave are $A$, and the frequency is $\nu$. Find the equation for...

A car of rests on a slanted ramp attached to a trailer as shown in figure. Only a cable running from the trailer to the car prevents the car from rolling off the ramp. (The car's brakes are off and its transmission is in neutral.) Find the force that the ramp exerts on the car's tires.

The pulley in the figure has radius \(0.160\text{ m}\) and moment of inertia \(0.380\text{ kg}\cdot\text{m}^2\). The rope does not slip on the pulley rim. Calculate the speed of the \(4.00\text{ kg}\) block just before it strikes the floor.

A 0.150 kg frame, when suspended from a coil spring, stretches the spring 0.0400 m. A 0.200 kg lump of putty is dropped from rest onto the frame as shown in figure. Find the maximum distance the frame moves downward from its initial equilibrium position.

The rocket accelerates from rest to $v = \sqrt{0.9999}c$ as shown in figure. Assume the instantaneous acceleration in the inertial frame is $|\dot{v}| = 20 \text{ m/s}^2$. According to the clocks inside the rocket, how much time does it take to accelerate the rocket?

Two rays traveling parallel to the principal axis strike a large plano-convex lens having a refractive index of 1.60 as shown in the figure. If the convex face is spherical, a ray near the edge does not pass through the focal point (spherical aberration occurs). Assume this face has a radius of curvature of \( R = 20.0 \, \text{cm} \) and the two rays are at distances \( h_1 = 0.500 \, \text{cm} \) and \( h_2 = 12.0 \, \text{cm} \) from the principal axis. Find the difference \( \Delta x \) i...

An enemy spacecraft moves at a speed of \( v = 0.800c \) as shown in figure. A galactic patrol spacecraft pursues at a speed of \( u = 0.900c \) relative to the Earth. Observers on the Earth measure the patrol craft to be overtaking the enemy craft at a relative speed of \( 0.100c \). With what speed is the patrol craft overtaking the enemy craft as measured by the patrol craft's crew?

Owen and Dina are at rest in frame S', which is moving with respect to frame S. They play a game of catch while Ed, at rest in frame S, watches the action as shown in the figure. Owen throws the ball to Dina. What time interval is required for the ball to reach Dina?

A gate weighs \(700\,\text{N}\). Its center of gravity is at its center. To relieve the strain on the top hinge, a wire is connected as shown in the figure. The tension in \(CD\) is increased until the horizontal force at hinge \(A\) is zero. What is the combined vertical force exerted by hinges \(A\) and \(B\)?

Four parallel copper wires of size 10 are arranged such that their cross-sections form a square. Each wire carries a current, as shown in Figure. What are the magnitude and direction of the force F per meter experienced by the bottom-left wire?

A wedge with mass \( M \) rests on a frictionless, horizontal tabletop. A block with mass \( m \) is placed on the wedge, and a horizontal force \( \vec{F} \) is applied to the wedge as shown in the figure. What must the magnitude of \( \vec{F} \) be if the block is to remain at a constant height above the tabletop?

A string is wrapped several times around the rim of a small hoop of mass \(0.180\text{ kg}\). The free end of the string is held in place, and the hoop is released from rest as shown in the figure. After the hoop has descended \(75.0\text{ cm}\), calculate the speed of its center.

Vector \( \vec{A} \) has magnitude 6 units and is in the direction of the \( +x \)-axis. Vector \( \vec{B} \) has magnitude 4 units and lies in the xy-plane. Find the vector product \( \vec{C} = \vec{A} \times \vec{B} \).

final_category_zh: 近代物理、量子与相对论
question: Figure shows dust particles (\( m = 1.0 \times 10^{-15} \, \text{kg} \)) in a vacuum chamber. The dust particles are released from rest above a hole, fall through the hole (there's just barely room for the particles to go through), and land on a detector below. Quantum effects would be noticeable if the detection-circle diameter increased by \(10\%\) to \(1.1 \, \mu\text{m}\). At what distance \(d\) would the detector need to be placed to observe this increase in the diameter?

An inventor designs a pendulum clock using a bob with mass \( m \) at the end of a thin wire. Instead of swinging back and forth, the bob is to move in a horizontal circle at constant speed as shown in the figure. Find the period \( T \) (the time for one revolution of the bob).

Figure shows a 2.0-cm-diameter solenoid passing through the center of a 6.0-cm-diameter loop. The magnetic field inside the solenoid is 0.20 T. What is the magnetic flux through the loop when it is tilted at a 60\(^{\circ}\) angle?

As shown in the figure, to measure the thickness of a certain transparent thin film coated on silicon nitride, a part of it is etched away to form a wedge. It is known that the refractive index of the transparent thin film is 1.76, the refractive index of silicon nitride is 2.35, and the wavelength of the incident light is 589 nm. Find the thickness of the transparent thin film, $e$.

The biggest stuffed animal in the world is a snake \(420\ \text{m}\) long, constructed by Norwegian children. Suppose the snake is laid out in a park as shown in the figure, forming two straight sides of a \(105^\circ\) angle, with one side \(240\ \text{m}\) long. Olaf and Inge run a race they invent. Inge runs directly from the tail of the snake to its head, and Olaf starts from the same place at the same moment but runs along the snake. If Inge runs the race again at a constant speed of \(1...

An object at $2.28 \text{ m}$ in front of the camera lens $L$ is sharply imaged on the photographic plate $P$, which is $12.0 \text{ cm}$ behind the lens. Now, a parallel glass plate with a refractive index of 1.50 and a thickness of $AB = 0.90 \text{ cm}$ is inserted between the lens and the photographic plate, and the glass plate is perpendicular to the optical axis, as shown in Figure. Assume that the camera lens can be regarded as a simple thin convex lens, and the light rays are paraxial...

As shown in the figure, a drop of oil falls on a flat glass plate, forming an oil film whose upper surface is approximately spherical. It is known that the refractive index of the oil film is $n_2 = 1.2$, the thickness of the oil film at the center is $e_{\text{m}} = 1.2\ \mu\text{m}$, and the refractive index of the glass is $n_3 = 1.5$. If monochromatic parallel light with wavelength $\lambda = 550\ \text{nm}$ is incident perpendicularly on the oil film, how many complete dark rings can be...

Let the earth and spaceship reference frames be \( S \) and \( S' \), respectively. Their relative velocity is \( u = 0.900c \). The probe velocity with respect to \( S' \) is \( v'_x = 0.700c \). Find the probe velocity \( v_x \) relative to \( S \).

A beaker contains water as shown in the figure. There is a coin at the bottom of the beaker, and a thin lens with a focal length of $30 \text{ cm}$ is placed on the water surface. The center of the coin is on the optical axis of the lens. If the observer above the lens (looking through the lens) sees the coin still at its original position, where should the lens be placed, relative to the water surface? (Given that the refractive index of water is $4/3$, and neglect the thickness of the coin).

Figure shows a top view of a square enclosure. The inner surfaces are plane mirrors. A ray of light enters a small hole in the center of one mirror. At what angle \( \theta \) must the ray enter if it exits through the hole after being reflected once by each of the other three mirrors?

A motorist traveling at a constant speed passes a school crossing where the speed limit is \(10\,\text{m/s}\) (\(36\,\text{km/h}\), or about \(22\,\text{mi/h}\)). Just as the motorist passes the school-crossing sign, a police officer on a motorcycle stopped there starts in pursuit with constant acceleration as shown in the figure. How much time elapses before the officer passes the motorist?

Suppose that a particle with mass \( m \) and energy \( E \) is moving toward the origin of a system \( S \) such that its velocity \( \mathbf{u} \) makes an angle \( \alpha \) with the \( y \) axis as shown in figure. determine the energy \( E' \) of the particle measured by an observer in \( S' \), which moves relative to \( S \) so that the particle moves along the \( y' \) axis.

As shown in Figure, two containers with volumes $V_1 = V_2 = V$ are connected by a small tube with a valve. Initially, the valve is closed, and both containers contain monatomic gas with pressures $P_1$ and $P_2$ and temperatures $T_1$ and $T_2$ respectively. After the valve is opened, what are the final pressure $P_f$ of the gas in the total system? (Ignore heat loss from the system.)

An observer \( O \) is standing on a platform on a space station. A rocket passes at a relative moving parallel to the edge of the platform. The observer \( O \) notes that the front and back of the rocket simultaneously line up with the ends of the platform at a particular instant as shown in figure. According to \( O' \), how long does it take for observer \( O \) to pass the entire length of the rocket?

You are doing exercises on a Nautilus machine in a gym to strengthen your deltoid (shoulder) muscles. Your arms are raised vertically and can pivot around the shoulder joint. The deltoid muscle is attached to the humerus \(15.0\, \text{cm}\) from the shoulder joint. If you have set the tension in the cable of the machine to \(36.0\, \text{N}\) on each arm, what is the tension in each deltoid muscle if you simply hold your outstretched arms in place?
The switch in figure has been open for a long time. It is closed at \( t = 0 \, \text{s} \). After the switch has been closed for a long time, what is the current in the circuit?

As shown in the figure, a converging lens with a focal length of 50 cm is divided into two halves from the middle, and the two halves are separated by a distance of 1 mm. A light source is placed 300 cm in front of the lens, and the screen is 450 cm away from the lens. When light with a wavelength of 0.5 μm is emitted from the light source S and passes through the lens, find the fringe spacing.

A sound source S (with an emitted frequency of 2040 Hz) approaches a reflecting surface with velocity $v_s$ (see figure). An observer at point A hears a beat frequency of $\Delta\nu = 3$ Hz. Find the velocity $v_s$ of the sound source. Assume the speed of sound is 340 m/s.

Consider a light ray traveling between air and a diamond cut in the shape shown in the figure. If the light ray entering the diamond remains vertical as shown in the figure, what angle of rotation should the diamond in the water be rotated about an axis perpendicular to the page through \( O \) so that light will first exit the diamond at \( P \)?

A parallel-plate capacitor has a capacitance of 100 pF (1 pF = 10^-12 F). The separation between the plates is 1 cm. It is charged by a battery to a potential difference of 10 V. Then the capacitor is disconnected. The lower plate is illuminated with blue light, causing electrons with kinetic energy from 0 to 1.5 eV to be emitted. The potential difference attracts these electrons to the upper plate. The total current as a function of time is shown in the figure. After what time (t1 in the fig...
The cone has mass \(M\). Calculate the moment of inertia of a uniform solid cone about an axis through its center as shown in the figure.

An adventurous archaeologist crosses between two rock cliffs by slowly going hand over hand along a rope stretched between the cliffs. He stops to rest at the middle of the rope as shown in the figure. The rope will break if the tension in it exceeds \(2.50 \times 10^4\) N, and our hero’s mass is 90.0 kg. What is the smallest value \(\theta\) can have if the rope is not to break?
Given a plane cosine wave propagating along the positive x-axis, its waveform at $t = \frac{1}{3} s$ is shown in the figure, and the period is $T = 2 s$. Find the distance of point P from point O.

In a set of experiments on a hypothetical one-electron atom, you measure the wavelengths of the photons emitted from transitions ending in the ground state \( n = 1 \), as shown in the energy-level diagram in the figure. You also observe that it takes \( 17.50 \, \text{eV} \) to ionize this atom. If an electron made a transition from the \( n = 4 \) to the \( n = 2 \) level, what wavelength of light would it emit?

In a simple model for a radioactive nucleus, an alpha particle, \( m = 6.64 \times 10^{-27} \, \text{kg} \), is trapped by a square barrier that has width \( 2.0 \, \text{fm} \) and height \( 30.0 \, \text{MeV} \). What is the tunneling probability when the alpha particle encounters the barrier if its kinetic energy is \( 1.0 \, \text{MeV} \) below the top of the barrier, as shown in the figure?

Your cousin Throckmorton skateboards from rest down a curved, frictionless ramp. If we treat Throcky and his skateboard as a particle, he moves through a quarter-circle as shown in figure. Throcky and his skateboard have a total mass of 25.0 kg. Find his speed at the bottom of the ramp.

The ladder weighs \(3400\,\text{N}\) and its center of gravity is at its center. The ladder is pivoted at one end (A) about a pin; ignore the friction torque at the pin. The ladder is raised into position by a force applied by a hydraulic piston at C. The force \(\vec{F}\) exerted by the piston makes an angle of \(40^\circ\) with the ladder. What magnitude must \(\vec{F}\) have to just lift the ladder off the support bracket at B?

A box resting on a horizontal, frictionless surface is attached to a weight by a thin, light wire that passes over a frictionless pulley, as shown in the figure. The pulley has the shape of a uniform solid disk of mass \(2.00\text{ kg}\) and diameter \(0.500\text{ m}\). After the system is released, find the acceleration of the box.

In figure, \( m_1 = 20.0 \text{ kg} \) and \( \alpha = 53.1^\circ \). The coefficient of kinetic friction between the block of mass \( m_1 \) and the incline is \( \mu_{\text{k}} = 0.40 \). What must be the mass \( m_2 \) of the hanging block if it is to descend \( 12.0 \text{ m} \) in the first \( 3.00 \text{ s} \) after the system is released from rest?
In a laboratory, electron A is measured to move to the right, and electron B is measured to move to the left as shown in the figure. What is the velocity of electron A relative to electron B?

A uniform, \( 255\, \text{N} \) rod that is \( 2.00\, \text{m} \) long carries a weight at its right end and an unknown weight \( W \) toward the left end. When \( W \) is placed \( 50.0\, \text{cm} \) from the left end of the rod, the system just balances horizontally when the fulcrum is located \( 75.0\, \text{cm} \) from the right end. Find \( W \).
An infinite square well, shown in figure, is perturbed by an additional potential energy term, with the resultant well shown in figure. Find the first-order corrections to the energies.

A solid uniform ball rolls without slipping up a hill as shown in figure. At the top of the hill, it is moving horizontally, and then it goes over the vertical cliff. Neglect rolling friction and assume the system’s total mechanical energy is conserved. How fast is it moving just before it lands?

The starships of the Solar Federation are marked with the symbol of the federation, a circle, while starships of the Denebian Empire are marked with the empire's symbol, an ellipse whose major axis is 1.40 times longer than its minor axis as shown in the figure. How fast, relative to an observer, does an empire ship have to travel for its marking to be confused with the marking of a federation ship?

An electron with kinetic energy \( E = 5.00 \text{ eV} \) is incident on a barrier of width \( L = 0.200 \text{ nm} \) and height \( U = 10.0 \text{ eV} \) as shown in figure. What is the probability that the electron tunnels through the barrier?

Assume a ring with mass $m$ is fixed on an axis PP' which is coplanar with it and tangent to its circumference. The ring can oscillate freely in and out of the plane of the paper, as shown in Figure. Find the period $T_2$ for small oscillations of this ring.

A door can rotate freely about a vertical axis through its hinges. A bullet strikes the door as shown in the figure, in a direction perpendicular to the plane of the door, and embeds itself there. Find the door's angular speed.

spaceship travels parallel to the ground. A rod is placed on the spaceship along the direction of motion. An observer on the ground measures the length of the rod as shown in figure. Find the proper length $l_0$ of the rod.

Your firm needs to haul granite blocks up a slope out of a quarry and to lower dirt into the quarry to fill the holes. You design a system in which a granite block on a cart with steel wheels (weight \( w_1 \), including both block and cart) is pulled uphill on steel rails by a dirt-filled bucket (weight \( w_2 \), including both dirt and bucket) as shown in the figure. How must the weights \( w_1 \) and \( w_2 \) be related in order for the system to move with constant speed? Ignore friction...

As shown in the figure, this is a GaAs light-emitting diode. To increase the output optical power, a layer of anti-reflection coating with a refractive index of $n_2=1.38$ is coated on its hemispherical inner surface. It is known that the wavelength of light emitted by GaAs is $\lambda=930 \text{ nm}$ and its refractive index is $n_1=3.4$. Find the minimum thickness $e_0$ of the anti-reflection coating.

A highway curve forms a section of a circle. A car goes around the curve as shown in the helicopter view of figure. Its dashboard compass shows that the car is initially heading due east. After it travels \( d = 840 \text{ m} \), it is heading \( \theta = 35.0^\circ \) south of east. Find the radius of curvature of its path.
The metal wire in the figure moves relative to a straight wire. Find an expression for the potential difference between the two ends of the moving wire.

The figure shows the probability density for an electron that has passed through an experimental apparatus. If \(1.0 \times 10^6\) electrons are used, what is the expected number that will land in a \(0.010\text{-mm}\) wide strip at \(x = 2.000\text{ mm}\)?

A screen has two parallel slits of width $a = 6 \text{ cm}$, with a separation between the slits of $d = 12 \text{ cm}$, as shown in Figure. Microwave radiation with wavelength $\lambda = 3 \text{ cm}$ is incident on the screen. Calculate the transmitted diffraction pattern $I(\theta)/I_{\text{max}}$.

Two volumes are separated by a stopcock. At time $t=0$, the stopcock is opened. $V_2 = 2V_1$ as shown in the figure. After a long time, for the following case $V_1$ contains 1 mole of nitrogen gas, and $V_2$ contains 2 moles of nitrogen gas. Calculate the change in entropy of the gas, $\Delta S$.

The plates of a parallel-plate capacitor are squares with side length 20 cm. The separation between the plates is 1 cm. The capacitor is charged to a potential difference of 10 V and then disconnected from the battery. As shown in the figure, a large square dielectric plate with thickness slightly less than 1 cm is inserted between the two plates, so that an area of 10 cm × 20 cm within the capacitor is filled by the dielectric. The dielectric constant of the dielectric is 4.0. At positions i...

In an electron tube, electrons are emitted from a hot metal plate (emitter) and collected by a metal plate (collector) parallel to the emitter. The two metal plates are separated by a distance $d$. As shown in Figure. (Compared to the lateral dimensions of the metal plates, the distance $d$ is very small.) The potential between the two plates is given by $\phi = kx^{4/3}$, where $x$ is the distance from the emitter. For $0 < x < d$, what is the volume charge density $\rho(x)$?

Mountaineers often use a rope to lower themselves down the face of a cliff (this is called \textit{rappelling}). They do this with their body nearly horizontal and their feet pushing against the cliff. Suppose that an \(82.0\, \text{kg}\) climber, who is \(1.90\, \text{m}\) tall and has a center of gravity \(1.1\, \text{m}\) from his feet, rappels down a vertical cliff with his body raised \(35.0^\circ\) above the horizontal. He holds the rope \(1.40\, \text{m}\) from his feet, and it makes a...

As shown in figure, assume that a photon manifest any increase in its energy by changing its frequency. Find the frequency of a photon of frequency $\nu$ after falling through a height $H$ in earth's gravitational field.

A student stands at the edge of a cliff and throws a stone horizontally over the edge with a speed of \( v_i = 18.0 \text{ m/s} \). The cliff is \( h = 50.0 \text{ m} \) above a body of water as shown in figure. With what speed does the stone land?

The discovery of the antiproton \( \overline{\text{p}} \) (a particle with the same rest energy as a proton, \( 938 \text{ MeV} \), but with the opposite electric charge) took place in 1956 through the following reaction: \[ \text{p} + \text{p} \rightarrow \text{p} + \text{p} + \text{p} + \overline{\text{p}} \] in which accelerated protons were incident on a target of protons at rest in the laboratory. The minimum incident kinetic energy needed to produce the reaction is called the \textit{th...

A plate is attached to the lower end of a vertically suspended light spring (as shown in the figure). An object of mass m falls freely from a height above the plate and lands on the plate without rebounding. Taking the instant the object lands on the plate as the origin of time. Find the equation of motion of the plate. (Take the equilibrium position after the object lands on the plate as the origin of the coordinate system, with downward displacement being positive.)

Assume a photomultiplier tube for detecting radiation has seven dynodes with potentials of \(100, 200, 300, \ldots, 700 \text{ V}\) as shown in figure. The average energy required to free an electron from the dynode surface is \(10.0 \text{ eV}\). Assume only one electron is incident and the tube functions with \(100\%\) efficiency. What is the energy available to the counter for all the electrons arriving at the last dynode?

A spin-\(1/2\) particle with a magnetic moment is prepared in the state \(|-\rangle_x\) and is subject to a uniform applied magnetic field \(\mathbf{B} = B_0 \hat{z}\). Find the probability of measuring spin up in the \(x\)-direction after a time \(t\). This experiment is depicted in figure.

When manufacturing semiconductor devices, it is often necessary to accurately measure the thickness of the silicon dioxide ($\text{SiO}_2$) thin film on a silicon wafer. At this point, a portion of the silicon dioxide thin film can be etched away to form a wedge, and its thickness can be measured using interference fringes of equal thickness. It is known that the refractive index of Si is 3.42, and the refractive index of $\text{SiO}_2$ is 1.5. When illuminated perpendicularly with a helium-n...

The spectrometer on the 150 ft tower solar telescope at Mount Wilson Observatory is of the Littrow type, as shown in the figure. In this setup, there is a lens that serves as both the collimating lens and the camera lens, and $\theta_i = -\theta_d$. The spectrum is formed in a narrow, long region near the slit. The focal length of the instrument is $F = 23 \, \text{m}$, the grating's ruled area is 15 cm × 25 cm, and it has 600 lines per millimeter. What value should the tilt angle $\theta$ of...

Blocks \( A \), \( B \), and \( C \) are placed as in figure and connected by ropes of negligible mass. Both \( A \) and \( B \) weigh 25.0 N each, and the coefficient of kinetic friction between each block and the surface is 0.35. Block \( C \) descends with constant velocity. If the rope connecting \( A \) and \( B \) were cut, what would be the acceleration of \( C \)?

In the reaction \( \text{K}^- + \text{p} \rightarrow \Lambda^0 + \pi^0 \), a charged K meson (mass \( 493.7 \text{ MeV}/c^2 \)) collides with a proton (\( 938.3 \text{ MeV}/c^2 \)) at rest, producing a lambda particle (\( 1115.7 \text{ MeV}/c^2 \)) and a neutral pi meson (\( 135.0 \text{ MeV}/c^2 \)), as represented in figure. The initial kinetic energy of the K meson is \( 152.4 \text{ MeV} \). After the interaction, the pi meson has a kinetic energy of \( 254.8 \text{ MeV} \). Find the kine...

A solid, uniform, spherical boulder starts from rest and rolls down a hill, as shown in the figure. The top half of the hill is rough enough to cause the boulder to roll without slipping, but the lower half is covered with ice and there is no friction. What is the translational speed of the boulder when it reaches the bottom of the hill? Neglect rolling friction and assume the system’s total mechanical energy is conserved.

Two identical cylinders, each with moment of inertia $I$, are suspended by identical steel wires with torsional constant $K$, as shown in the figure. No swinging is allowed. How many modes of vibration are there?

In the circuit shown in Figure, when t < 0, the switch is open, and the capacitor is uncharged. At the instant the voltage across the capacitor reaches 8.0 V, the switch is moved to B. What is the initial current I0 through L?

As shown in Figure, a "Dewar container" (for example, a thermos flask) is a double-walled vacuum container used for insulation. Assume the condition for the Dewar container to achieve good insulation is $l \ge 10D$. Here, $l$ is the mean free path of the residual gas between the double walls, and $D$ is the distance between the double walls. Assume $D = 1 \text{ cm}$, and the residual gas is oxygen. For pressures $P_1 = 400 \text{ mmHg}$ and $P_2 = 200 \text{ mmHg}$, what is the ratio of the...
A pendulum is made of a light rod and a uniform circular disk. The disk is rigidly connected to the rod as shown in the figure. When the pendulum undergoes small oscillations, find the period.

final_category_zh: 近代物理、量子与相对论
question: A photon scatters off a free electron at A as shown in the figure, producing a second photon. This photon then scatters off another free electron at B, producing a third photon. Determine the value of \(\Delta \lambda\).

An ideal conducting sphere of radius $R$ moves with a constant velocity $\mathbf{v} = v\mathbf{e}_x$ ($v \ll c$) in a uniform magnetic field $\mathbf{B} = B\mathbf{e}_y$, as shown in Figure. In the lowest order approximation of $v/c$. Write the induced surface charge distribution on the surface of the conductor in a reference frame in which the conductor is at rest.

You are designing an airplane propeller as shown in figure. The speed of the propeller tips through the air must not exceed \(270\ \text{m/s}\). (This is about \(80\%\) of the speed of sound in air. If the propeller tips moved faster, they would produce a lot of noise.) What is the maximum possible propeller radius?

As shown in Figure, a converging light beam originally converges at point $P$. After inserting a plane parallel glass plate with a refractive index of $n = 1.5$, the image point shifts to point $P'$. Find the thickness $t$ of the glass plate.

A lens with a focal length of 25 cm has its central portion of width $a = 3$ mm removed, and the remaining two parts are glued together. As shown in the figure, this type of lens is called a biprism lens. A monochromatic point source with a wavelength of 600.0 nm is placed on the left axis of this lens, and an observation screen is placed on the other side. During the observation of the interference fringes, it is found that the fringe spacing $\Delta x$ remains constant when the observation...

As some molten metal splashes, one droplet flies off to the east, and another droplet flies off to the west as shown in the figure. In terms of \(v_i\) and \(\theta_i\), find the distance between the two droplets as a function of time.

The figure shows a sound interferometer used to demonstrate sound wave interference. S is a loudspeaker, and D is a sound detector, such as an ear or microphone. The length of path SBD can be varied, but the path SAD is fixed. There is air in the interferometer, and it is known that the sound intensity at the first position of B is a minimum value of 100 units. When B is gradually moved to the second position, 0.0165 m away from the first position, the intensity is a maximum value of 900 unit...

You place a book of mass 5.00 kg against a vertical wall. You apply a constant force \( \vec{F} \) to the book, where \( F = 96.0 \text{ N} \) as shown in figure. The coefficient of kinetic friction between the book and the wall is 0.300. If the book is initially at rest, what is its speed after it has traveled 0.400 m up the wall?
The graph in figure shows the stopping potential as a function of the frequency of the incident light falling on a metal surface. Find the photoelectric work function for this metal.

A 500.0 g bird is flying horizontally at 2.25 m/s, not paying much attention, when it suddenly flies into a stationary vertical bar as shown in the figure. The bar is uniform, 0.750 m long, has a mass of 1.50 kg, and is hinged at its base. What is the angular velocity of the bar just as it reaches the ground?

Your camping buddy has an idea for a light to go inside your tent. He happens to have a powerful (and heavy!) horseshoe magnet that he bought at a surplus store. This magnet creates a field between two pole tips \(10\,\text{cm}\) apart. His idea is to build the hand-cranked generator shown in the figure. He thinks you can make enough current to fully light a lightbulb. That's not super bright, but it should be plenty of light for routine activities in the tent. With what frequency will you ha...

Refer to Figure. There is a line charge of length $(l_1 + l_2)\text{ m}$ with charge density $\lambda\text{ C/m}$. The distance from point $P$ to the line is $r$. Find the electric potential $\phi$ at point $P$.

From the Earth's coordinate system, a spacecraft is launched from $x = 0$ and accelerates along the x-axis. At time $t$, the spacecraft's position is $x(t)$ as shown in the figure. Assuming an astronaut in the spacecraft constantly monitors the acceleration and finds the acceleration to be a constant $g$. When the spacecraft's velocity reaches $v$, what is the distance traveled, $x$?
In the figure, the system is released from rest. In terms of \(m_1\), \(m_2\), \(m_3\), and \(g\), what is the tension in string \(C\)?

Two parallel and coherent light beams propagate parallel to the x-z plane and are symmetrically incident on the x-y plane (with the y-axis perpendicular to the paper), as shown in the figure, with a wavelength of $\lambda = 6328\,\text{\AA}$ and $\theta_1 = \theta_2 = \theta$. When $\theta = 30^\circ$, calculate the fringe spacing $\Delta x$ (between adjacent maxima or minima).

The figure shows a Rayleigh interferometer used to measure the refractive index of air. Two identical glass tubes T1 and T2 are placed behind the double slits. Initially, tube T1 is evacuated, and tube T2 is filled with the air to be measured. After the experiment begins, air is slowly introduced into tube T1 until the pressure in both tubes is equal. During the entire process, the intensity at point P was observed to change 98 times. The wavelength of the incident light is 589.3 nm, and the...

In a truck-loading station at a post office, a small package is released from rest at point A on a track that is one-quarter of a circle as shown in the figure. The size of the package is much less than 1.60 m, so the package can be treated as a particle. It slides down the track and reaches point B with a speed of 4.80 m/s. From point B, it slides on a level surface a distance of 3.00 m to point C, where it comes to rest. How much work is done on the package by friction as it slides down the...
As shown in Figure, six sliders, each of mass $m$, are connected by identical springs and are confined to move within a linear air track. How many independent modes of vibration are there?

A pendulum is made of a light rod and a uniform circular disk. The disk is mounted on a smooth pivot at the end of the rod, allowing it to rotate freely relative to it, as shown in the figure. When the pendulum undergoes small oscillations, find the period.

Experiments to study vision often need to track the movements of a subject's eye. One way of doing so is to have the subject sit in a magnetic field while wearing special contact lenses with a coil of very fine wire circling the edge. A current is induced in the coil each time the subject rotates his eye. Consider the experiment of the figure in which a 20-turn coil of wire circles the subject's cornea while a \(1.0\,\text{T}\) magnetic field is directed as shown. The subject begins by lookin...

We want to slide a 12 kg crate up a ramp inclined. A worker, ignoring friction, calculates that he can do this by giving it an initial speed at the bottom and letting it go. But friction is \textit{not} negligible; the crate slides up the ramp, stops, and slides back down as shown in figure. How fast is the crate moving when it reaches the bottom of the ramp?
As shown in Figure, there is a charge $+q$ at a distance from an uncharged conducting sphere. Find the electric potential $\phi$ of the sphere.

As shown in Figure, the relationship between the refractive index and the height $y$ of a glass block with thickness is: $n(y) = \frac{n_0}{1 - \frac{y}{r_0}}$ where $n_0 = 1.2$, $r_0 = 13 \text{ cm}$. A light ray enters along the x-axis from the origin. The angle of emergence at point A on the other side is $30^\circ$. The thickness $d$ of the glass.

In the Davisson-Germer experiment, 54.0-eV electrons were diffracted from a nickel lattice. If the first maximum in the diffraction pattern was observed at \(\phi = 50.0^\circ\) as shown in the figure, what was the lattice spacing \(a\) between the vertical columns of atoms?

As shown in figure, a light ray is incident normal to one face of a block of flint glass (a prism) that is immersed in water. A substance is dissolved in the water to increase the index of refraction \(n_2\). At what value of \(n_2\) does total internal reflection cease at point \(P\)?
A photon having energy \( E_0 = 0.880 \text{ MeV} \) is scattered by a free electron initially at rest as shown in the figure. Determine the momentum of the scattered electron.

A force \( \vec{F}_1 \) of magnitude 6.00 units acts on an object at the origin in a direction \( \theta = 30.0^\circ \) above the positive \( x \) axis as shown in the figure. A second force \( \vec{F}_2 \) of magnitude 5.00 units acts on the object. Find the magnitude of the resultant force \( \vec{F}_1 + \vec{F}_2 \).

A symmetric biconvex lens has radii of curvature of 3 cm for both surfaces as shown in the figure, and is made of glass with a refractive index of 1.50. The lens is placed on the surface of water. An object Q is located 4 cm below the lens. Calculate the position of the final image in air. (The refractive index of water is 1.33.)
As shown in Figure, two charged particles are located on the x-axis: Particle 1 is located at the origin; Particle 2 is located at $x = a$. Find the electromagnetic force exerted on particle 2.

A moving rod is observed to have a length of \( \ell = 2.00 \, \text{m} \) and to be oriented at an angle of \( \theta = 30.0^\circ \) with respect to the direction of motion as shown in figure. The rod has a speed of \( 0.995c \). What is the orientation angle in the proper frame?

The central part with a width of $a$ of a thin lens with a focal length of $f' = 25 \text{cm}$ is removed, and it satisfies $a \ll f'$. Then the remaining two parts are glued together, as shown in Figure. A monochromatic point light source S with a wavelength of 500 nm is placed on one side of the lens, and interference fringes are observed on a screen placed on the other side. The distance between two adjacent fringes on the screen is $\Delta y = 0.25 \text{mm}$, and $\Delta y$ remains uncha...

A elevator with broken cables in a test rig is falling when it contacts a cushioning spring at the bottom of the shaft. The spring is intended to stop the elevator as shown in figure. During the motion a safety clamp applies a constant 17,000 N friction force to the elevator. What is the necessary force constant \( k \) for the spring?

A woman stands on a bathroom scale while riding in an elevator, as shown in the figure. The elevator is initially moving downward at \(10.0\ \mathrm{m/s}\); it slows to a stop with constant acceleration over a distance of \(25.0\ \mathrm{m}\). While the elevator is moving downward with decreasing speed, what is the reading on the scale?

A person is looking into an empty container as shown in the figure. When the container is completely filled with a fluid of index of refraction \(n\) and viewed from the same angle, the person can see the center of a coin at the middle of the container’s bottom. For what range of values of \(n\) will the center of the coin not be visible for any values of \(h\) and \(d\)?

An enemy spaceship is moving toward your starfighter with a speed, as measured in your frame, of \(0.400c\). The enemy ship fires a missile toward you at a speed of \(0.700c\) relative to the enemy ship as shown in the figure. If you measure that the enemy ship is \(8.00 \times 10^6\) km away from you when the missile is fired, how much time, measured in your frame, will it take the missile to reach you?

A small block with a mass of 0.0600 kg is attached to a cord passing through a hole in a frictionless, horizontal surface (figure. P6.71). The block is originally revolving at a distance of 0.40 m from the hole with a speed of 0.70 m/s. The cord is then pulled from below, shortening the radius of the circle in which the block revolves to 0.10 m. At this new distance, the speed of the block is 2.80 m/s. How much work was done by the person who pulled on the cord?

If a classical system does not have a constant charge-to-mass ratio throughout the system, the magnetic moment can be written \[ \mu = g \frac{Q}{2M} L \] where \(Q\) is the total charge, \(M\) is the total mass, and \(g \neq 1\). What is \(g\) for a solid sphere (\(I = 2MR^2/5\)) that has a ring of charge on the surface at the equator, as shown in the figure?

As shown in Figure, the central thickness of the lens is $\overline{OO'} = 20\ \text{cm}$, the refractive index is $n = 1.5$, and the radii of curvature of the front and rear surfaces are 20 cm and 40 cm, respectively. The rear surface of the lens is coated with a silver reflective film. An object with height $y = 1\ \text{mm}$ is placed at point $Q$, which is 20 cm to the left of point $O$ on the front surface. Under the paraxial condition, find the height of the final image formed by the lens.

As shown in the figure, G1 and G2 are two gauge blocks (a gauge block is a steel rectangular block with two end faces ground and polished flat to be mutually parallel). G1 has a standard length, and G2 is a replicate of the same specification to be calibrated (the length difference between the two is exaggerated in the figure). G1 and G2 are placed on a platform and pressed down by a master flat glass plate T. Assume the wavelength of the normally incident light is λ = 589.3 nm, the separatio...

For the lifting equipment shown in the figure, the mass of the heavy object is $1.5 \times 10^4$ kg. When the heavy object is lowering uniformly at a speed of $v = 15$ m/min, the machine malfunctions and the steel cable is suddenly stopped. At this time, the steel cable is equivalent to a spring with a stiffness coefficient $k = 5.78 \times 10^6$ N/m. Find the maximum tension in the steel cable caused by the vibration of the heavy object.

As shown in the figure, a metal wire of mass $m$ slides frictionlessly on two rails. The rails are located in a vertical uniform magnetic field $B$. A constant current $I$ flows from generator $G$ into the rails and back along the other rail. Find the velocity of the wire as a function of time $v(t)$. (At $t=0$, the wire is at rest.)

Calibrating optical instruments requires determining the precise separation $y$ between two parallel lines. The measurement is performed as follows: two parallel light rays are incident perpendicularly and symmetrically onto a glass rod of refractive index $n = 1.60$. Adjust the distance between the two parallel lines until they precisely focus at a point on the opposite side of the circumference of the glass rod, as shown in the figure. Express $y$ in terms of $R$.

A playground is on the flat roof of a city school, \(6.00\ \text{m}\) above the street below as shown in the figure. The vertical wall of the building is \(h = 7.00\ \text{m}\) high, forming a 1-m-high railing around the playground. A ball has fallen to the street below, and a passerby returns it by launching it at an angle of \(\theta = 53.0^\circ\) above the horizontal from a point \(d = 24.0\ \text{m}\) from the base of the building wall. The ball takes \(2.20\ \text{s}\) to reach a point...

A waitress shoves a ketchup bottle to her right along a smooth, level lunch counter as shown in the figure. The bottle leaves her hand, then slows down as it slides because of a constant horizontal friction force exerted on it by the countertop. What is the magnitude of the friction force acting on the bottle?

A material is surrounded by vacuum and is in the shape of a quarter circle as shown in the figure. A light ray parallel to the base of the material is incident from the left and emerges from the material at the angle \(\theta\). Determine an expression for \(\theta\) in terms of \(n\), \(R\), and \(L\).

As shown in Figure, a thin convex lens $L_1$ has an image screen 15 cm to its right. Keeping the object and $L_1$ stationary, a thin concave lens $L_2$ with a focal length $f_2 = -20$ cm can be inserted after $L_1$ so that an image that is magnified by a factor of 2 is finally formed on the screen. What is the distance from $L_2$ to $L_1$?

A load of bricks hangs from one end of a rope that passes over a small, frictionless pulley. A counterweight is suspended from the other end of the rope as shown in figure. The system is released from rest. What is the tension in the rope while the load is moving?

final_category_zh: 近代物理、量子与相对论
question: Characteristic X-rays of frequency $4.2 \times 10^{18} \text{ Hz}$ are produced when transitions from $L$ shell to $K$ shell take place in a certain target material. Use Moseley's law to determine the atomic number of the target material. Given Rydberg constant $R = 1.1 \times 10^{7} \text{ m}^{-1}$.

A train is traveling through a (rest) length $L_0$ tunnel as shown in gigure. Find the time experienced by the passenger from the time the front of the train enters the tunnel to the time the tail of the train exits the tunnel.

final_category_zh: 近代物理、量子与相对论
question: A hypothetical atom is shown in figure. What is the highest frequencies of the spectral lines this atom can emit when excited?

A plane wave propagates in a medium with velocity $u = 20 \text{ m/s}$ along the negative x-axis direction. The oscillation equation for point $a$ is known to be $y_a = 3\cos 4\pi t$, where the unit of $t$ is s and the unit of $y$ is m. Write the wave equation with point $a$ as the coordinate origin.

Two identical spherical thin-shell glass hemispheres are glued together, with air in between. One hemisphere's surface is silvered to act as a spherical mirror, as shown in the figure. Using the "pinhole method", the light from the light bulb passes through the pinhole on the white screen. By adjusting $L$, a clear image of the pinhole is obtained on the white screen, and it is measured that $L = 20 \text{ cm}$. If the shell is filled with water with a refractive index of $4/3$, what is the v...

A thin, light string is wrapped around the outer rim of a uniform hollow cylinder of mass \(4.75\text{ kg}\) having inner and outer radii as shown in figure. The cylinder is then released from rest. How far must the cylinder fall before its center is moving at \(6.66\text{ m/s}\)?

A particle of mass $m$ and kinetic energy $E > 0$ approaches an abrupt potential drop $V_0$. When a free neutron enters a nucleus, it experiences a sudden drop in potential energy, from $V = 0$ outside to around $-12 \, \text{MeV}$ inside. Suppose a neutron, emitted with kinetic energy $4 \, \text{MeV}$ by a fission event, strikes such a nucleus. What is the probability it will be absorbed, thereby initiating another fission?
What is the electric potential at the point indicated with the dot? What is the electric potential at the point indicated with the dot?

Suppose three protons lie on the \(x\)-axis, at rest relative to one another at a given instant of time, as in the figure. If proton \(q_3\) on the right is released while the others are held fixed in place, evaluate the proton's speed when \(r_0 = 2.00\,\mathrm{fm}\). (Note: \(1\,\mathrm{fm} = 10^{-15}\,\mathrm{m}\).)

Three charges lie along the \( x \)-axis as in figure. The positive charge \( q_1 = 15 \, \mu\mathrm{C} \) is at \( x \)-axis, and the positive charge \( q_2 = 6.0 \, \mu\mathrm{C} \) is at the origin. Where must a \emph{negative} charge \( q_3 \) be placed on the \( x \)-axis so that the resultant electric force on it is zero?

A insulated cylinder has a frictionless piston held against stops by an external force of 500 kN. The piston crosssectional area is 0.5 m^2, and the initial volume is 0.25 m^3. Gas in the cylinder is at 200 kPa, 100\circ\text{C}. A valve is now opened to a line at 1.2 MPa, 200\circ\text{C}, and gas flows in until the cylinder pressure just balances the external force, at which point the valve is closed. Use constant heat capacity to verify that the final temperature is 645 K, and find the tot...
What is the potential difference between A and B? What is the potential difference between A and B?

Three uniform spheres are fixed at the positions shown in the figure. If the spheres are in deep outer space and a \(0.0150\,\text{kg}\) particle is released from rest \(300\,\text{m}\) from the origin along a line \(45^\circ\) below the \(-x\)-axis, what will the particle’s speed be when it reaches the origin?

The left figure shows the waveform of a sinusoidal transverse wave at \(t = 0.10\,\mathrm{s}\). Particle \(P\) is located at the equilibrium position \(x = 1.0\,\mathrm{m}\), and particle \(Q\) is located at \(x = 4.0\,\mathrm{m}\). The right figure shows the vibration graph of particle \(Q\). How long did the wave travel between \(t = 0.10\,\mathrm{s}\) and \(t = 0.25\,\mathrm{s}\)?
Based on the figure. Determine the capacitive reactance of the capacitor.

A piston/cylinder has nitrogen gas at $750\,\text{K}$ and $1500\,\text{kPa}$, as shown in the figure. It is expanded in a polytropic process with $n = 1.2$ to $P = 750\,\text{kPa}$. Find the specific heat transfer in the process.

One end of a post weighing \( 400\, \text{N} \) and with height \( h \) rests on a rough horizontal surface with \( \mu_s = 0.30 \). The upper end is held by a rope fastened to the surface. If the force \( \vec{F} \) is applied at the midpoint of the post, what is the largest value it can have without causing the post to slip?

Consider an ideal air-standard Ericsson cycle that has an ideal regenerator, as shown in the figure. The high pressure is \(1.5\,\mathrm{MPa}\) and the cycle efficiency is \(60\%\). Heat is rejected in the cycle at a temperature of \(350\,\mathrm{K}\), and the cycle pressure at the beginning of the isothermal compression process is \(150\,\mathrm{kPa}\). Determine the turbine work per kilogram of air.

Two charges, \( q = 8.00\,\mu\text{C} \) and \( q' = -5.00\,\mu\text{C} \), move with speeds \( v = 9.00 \times 10^4\,\text{m/s} \) and \( v' = 6.50 \times 10^4\,\text{m/s} \) respectively. When the charges are at the positions shown, find the magnetic force that \( q' \) exerts on \( q \).

Determine the self-inductance of a toroidal solenoid with cross-sectional area \( A \) and mean radius \( r \), closely wound with \( N \) turns of wire on a nonmagnetic core. Assume that \( B \) is uniform across a cross section (that is, neglect the variation of \( B \) with distance from the toroid axis). Determine the self-inductance of a toroidal solenoid.
As shown in the figure What is the wavelength?

Two kilograms of water at $120 \, ^{\circ}\text{C}$ with a quality of $25\%$ has its temperature raised $20 \, ^{\circ}\text{C}$ in a constant-volume process as in figure. What is the heat transfer in the process?

Superheated vapor ammonia enters an insulated nozzle at 30\circ\text{C}, 1000 kPa with a low velocity and at a rate of 0.01 kg/s. The ammonia exits at 300 kPa with a velocity of 450 m/s. Determine the temperature (or quality, if saturated) of the nozzle?

Air at 35\circ\text{C}, 105 kPa flows in a 100-mm × 150-mm rectangular duct in a heating system. The mass flow rate is 0.015 kg/s. What are the velocity of the air flowing in the duct and the volume flow rate?

The battery has negligible internal resistance and \( \mathcal{E} = 48.0 \, \text{V} \). \( R_1 = R_2 = 4.00 \, \Omega \) and \( R_4 = 3.00 \, \Omega \). What must the resistance \( R_3 \) be for the resistor network to dissipate electrical energy at a rate of 295 W?

A radio station on the earth’s surface emits a sinusoidal wave with average total power 50\ \mathrm{kW} Assuming that the transmitter radiates equally in all directions above the ground (which is unlikely in real situations) find the electric-field and magnetic-field amplitudes \( E_\mathrm{max} \) and \( B_\mathrm{max} \) detected by a satellite 100\ \mathrm{km} from the antenna.
Henry gets into an elevator on the 50th floor of a building and it begins moving at t = 0 s. The figure shows his weight over the next 12 s. What is Henry's mass?

A light ray of wavelength \(589\,\mathrm{nm}\) (produced by a sodium lamp) traveling through air is incident on a smooth, flat slab of crown glass at an angle \(\theta_1\) of \(30.0^\circ\) to the normal, as sketched in the figure. How does the answer for \(\theta_3\) change if the ray enters water below the slab instead of air?

Unlike most other plants, a skunk cabbage can regulate its internal temperature (set at \( T = 22^\circ \mathrm{C} \)) by altering the rate at which it produces energy. If it becomes covered with snow, it can increase that production so that its thermal radiation melts the snow in order to re-expose the plant to sunlight. Let's model a skunk cabbage with a cylinder of height \( h = 5.0\ \mathrm{cm} \) and radius \( R = 1.5\ \mathrm{cm} \) and assume it is surrounded by a snow wall at temperat...
What is the thermal efficiency for the heat engine shown in the figure on a $p$-$V$ diagram?
A ray of light falls on a transparent sphere with centre at \( C \) as shown in the figure. The ray emerges from the sphere parallel to the line \( AB \). What is the refractive index of the sphere?

A particle with mass \( m \) and charge \( q \) starts from rest at the origin in uniform fields \( \vec{E} \) along \( +y \) and \( \vec{B} \) out of the page. The particle moves in a cycloidal path with radius of curvature at the top points equal to twice the corresponding \( y \)-coordinate. Applying Newton's second law at the top point and taking as given that the radius of curvature here equals \( 2y \), prove that the speed at this point is \( \frac{2E}{B} \).

Atmospheric air at−45\circ\text{C}, 60 kPa enters the front diffuser of a jet engine with a velocity of 900 km/h and a frontal area of 1 m^2. After adiabatic diffuser process, the velocity is 20 m/s. Find the maximum pressure possible.

The spacecraft in figure is moving past the earth at a constant speed v that is 0.92 times the speed of light. The astronaut measures the time interval between successive “ticks” of the spacecraft clock to be \delta t_0 = 1.0 s. What is the time interval \delta t that an earth observer measures between “ticks” of the astronaut’s clock?

A coil with 4 turns, radius \(0.800\ \text{cm}\), and resistance \(0.250\ \Omega\) is placed between the poles of an electromagnet whose field increases from zero at \(t = 0\); the current \(i\) induced in the coil as a function of time \(t\) is shown. Calculate the magnetic field at the location of the coil for \(t = 6.00\ \text{s}\).

Four identical mirrors are made to stand vertically to form a square arrangement as shown in a top view. A ray starts from the midpoint \( M \) of mirror \( AD \) and after two reflections reaches corner \( D \). What must be the angle \( \theta \)?

A uniform string has a mass \( M \) of \( 0.030\,0 \, \mathrm{kg} \) and a length \( L \) of \( 6.00 \, \mathrm{m} \). Tension is maintained in the string by suspending a block of mass \( m \) from one end. Find the time it takes the pulse to travel from the wall to the pulley. Neglect the mass of the hanging part of the string.

A beam of light is incident on a prism of a certain glass at an angle of \( \theta_1 = 30.0^\circ \), as shown in figure. If the index of refraction of the glass for violet light is \( 1.80 \), \( \phi_2 \), find the angle of incidence at the glass-air interface.

Air enters the turbocharger compressor of an automotive engine at 100 kPa, 30\circ\text{C} and exits at 200 kPa. The air is cooled by 50\circ\text{C} in an intercooler before entering the engine. The isentropic efficiency of the compressor is 75%. Determine the temperature of the air entering the engine.

A sheet that is made of plastic (n = 1.60) covers one slit of a double slit. When the double slit is illuminated by monochromatic light (vacuum 586 nm), the center of the screen appears dark rather than bright. What is the minimum thickness of the plastic?

An afterburner in a jet engine adds fuel after the turbine, thus raising the pressure and temperature due to the energy of combustion. Assume a standard condition of \( 800 \, \mathrm{K}, \, 250 \, \mathrm{kPa} \), after the turbine into the nozzle that exhausts at \( 95 \, \mathrm{kPa} \). Assume the afterburner adds \( 450 \, \mathrm{kJ/kg} \) to that state, with a rise in pressure for the same specific volume, and neglect any upstream effects on the turbine. Find the nozzle exit velocity a...

A boat is travelling in a river along the stream flowing with a speed 2 m/s. From this boat, a sound transmitter is lowered into the river through a rigid support. The wavelength of the sound emitted from the transmitter inside the water is 14.45 mm. Assume that attenuation of sound in water and air is negligible. What will be the frequency detected by a receiver kept inside the river downstream?

The conductor, carrying current $I$ to the left, has three segments: (1) a straight segment with length $L$ perpendicular to the plane of the figure, (2) a semicircle with radius $R$, and (3) another straight segment with length $L$ parallel to the $x$-axis. Find the total magnetic force on this conductor.

A copper block of volume 1 L is heat treated at 500\circ\text{C} and now cooled in a 200-L oil bath initially at 20\circ\text{C}. Assuming no heat transfer with the surroundings, what is the final temperature?

\text{White light is incident onto a prism at the angle shown in figure. Violet light emerges perpendicular to the rear face of the prism. The index of refraction of violet light in this glass is } 2.0\% \text{ larger than the index of refraction of red light.} At what angle \phi does red light emerge from the rear face?

Figure shows the Diesel cycle. It is similar to the Otto cycle (see Problem CP19.71), but there are two important differences. First, the fuel is not admitted until the air is fully compressed at point 2. Because of the high temperature at the end of an adiabatic compression, the fuel begins to burn spontaneously. (There are no spark plugs in a diesel engine!) Second, combustion takes place more slowly, with fuel continuing to be injected. This makes the ignition stage a constant-pressure pro...
The figure shows the angular acceleration graph of a turntable that starts from rest. What is the turntable’s angular velocity at t = 3 s?

Figure gives \( \alpha \) versus the sine of the angle \( \theta \) in a single-slit diffraction experiment using light of wavelength 610\,\text{nm}. The vertical axis scale is set by \( \alpha_s = 12\,\text{rad} \). What is the slit width?
In the figure, a proton is fired with a speed of 200,000 m/s from the midpoint of the capacitor toward the positive plate. What is the proton’s speed as it collides with the negative plate?
Based on the figure How long is the streak of reflected light across the floor?

A longitudinal standing wave can be created in a long, thin aluminum rod by stroking the rod with very dry fingers. This is often done as a physics demonstration, creating a high-pitched, very annoying whine. From a wave perspective, the standing wave is equivalent to a sound standing wave in an open-open tube. As figure shows, both ends of the rod are anti-nodes. What is the fundamental frequency of a $2.0 \, \text{m}$-long aluminum rod?

The figure shows an accelerometer, a device for measuring the horizontal acceleration of cars and airplanes. A ball is free to roll on a parabolic track described by the equation y= x^2 , where both x and y are in meters. A scale along the bottom is used to measure the ball’s horizontal position x. What is the acceleration if x = 20 cm?

Figure shows a conducting loop consisting of a half-circle of radius \( r = 0.20 \, \mathrm{m} \) and three straight sections. The half-circle lies in a uniform magnetic field \( \vec{B} \) directed out of the page. The field magnitude is given by \( B = 4.0t^2 + 2.0t + 3.0 \), with \( B \) in teslas and \( t \) in seconds. An ideal battery with emf \( \mathcal{E}_{\mathrm{bat}} = 2.0 \, \mathrm{V} \) is connected to the loop. The resistance of the loop is \( 2.0 \, \Omega \). What is the mag...

Figure A shows the waveform of a sinusoidal transverse wave at a certain moment. The horizontal coordinates of particles a and b are x_a = 2 m and x_b = 6 m, respectively. Figure B shows the vibration graph of particle b, starting from that moment. What is the speed of the mass point at t = 2 s?

A steel tank of cross-sectional area 3 \m^2\ and height 16 m weighs 10 000 kg and is open at the top, as shown in the figure. We want to float it in the ocean so that it is positioned straight down by pouring concrete into its bottom. How much concrete should we use?

Two cylindrical cans with radius \( r = 5.00 \, \text{cm} \) and length \( L = 3.00 \, \text{cm} \) are filled with distilled water and a saltwater solution. A battery supplies \( 10.0 \, \text{V} \), and the resistor \( R = 15.0 \, \Omega \) is connected in series. The resistivity of the saltwater is given by \( ho = \frac{s_0}{s} \, \Omega \cdot \text{m} \), where \( s_0 = 6.30 \, \text{ppt} \) and \( s \) is the salinity in ppt. For what salinity level would the \( 15.0 \, \Omega \) resist...
This problem involves calculating the thermal efficiency and the heat extracted from the hot reservoir for a heat engine operating in a closed $p$-$V$ cycle. What is the thermal efficiency?

Two tanks contain steam and they are both connected to a piston/cylinder. Initially the piston is at the bottom, and the mass of the piston is such that a pressure of 1.4 MPa below it will be able to lift it. Stream in A is 4 kg at 7 MPa, 700\circ\text{C} and B has 2 kg at 3 MPa, 350\circ\text{C}. The two valves are opened, and the water comes to a uniform state. Find the total entropy generation, assuming no heat transfer.

When a system is taken from state $a$ to state $b$ in \textbf{figure}, 90.0~J of heat flows along path $acb$, and 60.0~J of work is done by the system. How much heat flows into the system along path $adb$ if the work done by the system is 15.0J?

A concave mirror of radius \( R \) is kept on a horizontal table as shown in the figure. Water (refractive index \( \mu \)) is poured into it up to a height \( h \). Where should an object be placed so that its image is formed on itself?

The normal human eye has maximum visual acuity with a pupil diameter of about 3\ \mathrm{mm}. For larger pupils, acuity decreases due to increasing aberrations; for smaller pupils, acuity decreases due to increasing diffraction. If your pupil diameter is 2.0\ \mathrm{mm}, as it would be in bright light, what is the smallest-diameter circle that you should be able to see as a circle, rather than just an unresolved blob, on an eye chart at the standard distance of 20\ \mathrm{ft}?
80 J of work are done on the gas in the process shown in figure. What is $V_1$ in $\text{cm}^3$?

A sinusoidal transverse wave with a period of \(2.0\,\mathrm{s}\) propagates along the \(x\)-axis. At a certain moment, the wave profile is as shown in the figure, and at this moment, particle \(P\) is moving in the negative direction of the \(y\)-axis. What is the speed of the wave?

A jet-ejector pump is a device in which a low-pressure (secondary) fluid is compressed by entrainment in a high-velocity (primary) fluid stream. The compression results from the deceleration in a diffuser. For purposes of analysis, this can be considered as equivalent to the turbine-compressor unit. Consider a steam jet pump with state 1 as saturated vapor at 35 kPa; state 3 is 300 kPa, 150◦C; and the discharge pressure, P_5, is 100 kPa. Determine the discharge temperature of the jet pump if...

One kilogram per second water enters a solar collector at 40^\circ\mathrm{C} and exits at 190^\circ\mathrm{C}, as shown in figure. The hot water is sprayed into a direct-contact heat exchanger (no mixing of the two fluids) used to boil the liquid butane. Pure saturated-vapor butane exits at the top at 80^\circ\mathrm{C} and is fed to the turbine. If the butane condenser temperature is 30^\circ\mathrm{C} and the turbine and pump isentropic efficiencies are each 80\%, determine the net power ou...

A flow of 0.02\,\mathrm{kmol/s} methane, CH_4, and 200\% theoretical air, both at reference conditions, are compressed separately to P_3 = P_4 = 2\,\mathrm{MPa}, then mixed and then burned in a steady-flow setup. After combustion, at state 6, heat transfer goes out, so the exhaust at state 7 is at 600\,\mathrm{K}. Find the rate of heat transfer minus the work terms (\dot{Q} - \dot{W}_1 - \dot{W}_2).

A vibrating string \(50.0\,\text{cm}\) long is under a tension of \(1.00\,\text{N}\). The results from five successive stroboscopic pictures are shown in the figure. The strobe rate is set at \(5000\) flashes per minute, and observations reveal that the maximum displacement occurred at flashes 1 and 5 with no other maxima in between. What is the mass of this string?

The battery has emf \(72.0\,\text{V}\) with negligible internal resistance. \(R_2 = 2.00\,\Omega\), \(C_1 = 3.00\,\mu\text{F}\), and \(C_2 = 6.00\,\mu\text{F}\). The charge on \(C_1\) is \(Q_1 = 18.0\,\mu\text{C}\). What is the final charge on \(C_2\)?
