Air flow at 100 kPa, 290 K, 100 m/s is directed toward a wall. There is no heat transfer at the wall. Find the stagnation pressure
Assume that the fluid is gasoline with atmospheric pressure 101 kPa on the top surface. What is the pressure at the bottom of the 7.5-m-tall storage tank of fluid at $25^{\circ}C$ shown in figure?

A steel cylinder has a cross-sectional area. At the bottom, is liquid water, on top of which is a layer of gasoline. This is shown in figure. The gasoline surface is exposed to atmospheric air at $101 \, \text{kPa}$. What is the highest pressure in the water?

The air column is made to vibrate in its second overtone by a tuning fork of frequency 440 Hz. The speed of sound in air is 330 m/s. End corrections may be neglected. Let $p_0$ denotes the mean pressure at any point in the pipe, and $\delt p_0$ the maximum amplitude of pressure variation. Find the length L of the air column.

A long wire carrying a current of \(4.50\text{A}\) makes two \(90^\circ\) bends as it passes through a uniform magnetic field of magnitude \(0.240\text{T}\), which is confined to a limited region of space. Find the magnitude of the force that the magnetic field exerts on the wire.

A metal bar of weight \(2.60\text{ N}\), length \(0.850\text{ m}\), and resistance \(10.0\Omega\) rests horizontally on conducting wires and lies in a uniform horizontal magnetic field of magnitude \(1.60\text{ T}\); the bar is not attached to the circuit. What is the acceleration of the bar just after the switch \(S\) is closed?

Both sections of the rod have cross-sectional areas of \( 4.00\,\text{cm}^2 \). The temperature of the copper–steel junction is \( 65.0^{\circ}\text{C} \) after a steady state has been set up. What is the length \( L_2 \) of the steel section?
As shown in the figure What is the magnitude of the puck’s acceleration at t = 5 s?

The hydraulic piston/cylinder system shown in the figure has a cylinder diameter of D=0.1 m with a piston and rod mass of 25 kg. The rod has a diameter of 0.01 m with an outside atmospheric pressure of 101 kPa. The inside hydraulic fluid pressure is 250 kPa. How large a force can the rod push with in the upward direction?

final_category_zh: 近代物理、量子与相对论
question: As shown in the figure. The center electron is displaced horizontally by a relatively small distance, what will its speed be when it is very far away?

A sinusoidal transverse wave has a wave speed of \(10\,\mathrm{m/s}\). Along the direction of propagation, two particles \(P\) and \(Q\) are located at \(x_P = 1\,\mathrm{m}\) and \(x_Q = 6\,\mathrm{m}\), respectively. Starting from the moment the wave reaches point \(P\), the vibration graph of that point is shown in the figure. What is the wavelength?

Consider three point charges at the corners of a triangle, as shown in the figure, where \( q_1 = 6.00 \times 10^{-9} \, \mathrm{C} \), \( q_2 = -2.00 \times 10^{-9} \, \mathrm{C} \), and \( q_3 = 5.00 \times 10^{-9} \, \mathrm{C} \). Find the magnitude of the resultant force on \( q_3 \).

Gasoline, C_7H_{17}, is burned in a steady-state burner with stoichiometric air at P_0, T_0, shown in figure. The gasoline is flowing as a liquid at T_0 to a carburetor, where it is mixed with air to produce a fuel air--gas mixture at T_0. The carburetor takes some heat transfer from the hot products to do the heating. After the combustion, the products go through a heat exchanger. The gasoline consumption is 10\,kg/h. How much power does the carburetor need?

A flow out of a clothes dryer of \( 0.05 \, \mathrm{kg/s} \) dry air is at \( 40^\circ \mathrm{C} \) and relative humidity \( 60\% \). It flows though a heat exchanger, where it exits at \( 20^\circ \mathrm{C} \). After exiting the heat exchanger, the flow combines with another flow of \( 0.03 \, \mathrm{kg/s} \) dry air at \( 30^\circ \mathrm{C} \) and relative humidity \( 30\% \). Find the heat transfer per kg dry air.

A U-shaped conductor is in a uniform magnetic field $\vec{B}$ perpendicular to the plane of the figure and directed into the page. A metal rod (the slidewire) of length $L$ is placed across the two arms of the conductor, forming a circuit, and moved to the right with constant velocity $\vec{v}$. Find the magnitude of the resulting induced emf.

A pair of glass slides \( 10.0 \, \mathrm{cm} \) long and with \( n = 1.52 \) are separated on one end by a hair, forming a triangular wedge of air, as illustrated in Figure 24.10. When coherent light from a helium--neon laser with wavelength \( 633 \, \mathrm{nm} \) is incident on the film from above, \( 15.0 \) dark fringes per centimeter are observed. How thick is the hair?

A flow out of a clothes dryer of \( 0.1 \, \mathrm{kg/s} \) dry air is at \( 60^\circ \mathrm{C} \) and relative humidity \( 60\% \). It flows through a heat exchanger, where it exits at \( 20^\circ \mathrm{C} \). After exiting the heat exchanger, the flow combines with another flow of \( 0.03 \, \mathrm{kg/s} \) dry air at \( 30^\circ \mathrm{C} \) and relative humidity \( 40\% \). Find the final exit state humidity ratio.

Semiconductors such as silicon are used to fabricate solar cells, devices that generate electric energy when exposed to sunlight. Solar cells are often coated with a transparent thin film to minimize reflective losses. A silicon solar cell is coated with a thin film of silicon monoxide for this purpose. Assuming normal incidence, determine the minimum thickness of the film that will produce the least reflection at a wavelength of \( 552 \, \mathrm{nm} \).

A piston/cylinder setup contains 1 kg of ammonia at 20\circ\text{C} with a volume of 0.1 m^3. Initially the piston rests on some stops with the top surface open to the atmosphere, $P_0$, so that a pressure of 1400 kPa is required to lift it. If it is heated to saturated vapor, find the final heat transfer.

A U-tube manometer filled with water (density = 1000 kg/m$^3$) shows a height difference of 25 cm. What is the gauge pressure? If the right branch is tilted to make an angle of $30^{\circ}$ with the horizontal, as shown in figure, what should the length of the column in the tilted tube be relative to the U-tube?
The figure shows a solid metal sphere at the center of a hollow metal sphere. What is the total charge on the inside surface of the hollow sphere?

A \( 0.2 \, \mathrm{m^3} \) insulated rigid vessel is divided into two equal parts, A and B, by an insulated partition, as shown in figure. The partition will support a pressure difference of \( 400 \, \mathrm{kPa} \) before breaking. Side A contains methane and side B contains carbon dioxide. Both sides are initially at \( 1 \, \mathrm{MPa}, \, 30^\circ \mathrm{C} \). A valve on side B is opened, and carbon dioxide flows out. The carbon dioxide that remains in B is assumed to undergo a rever...

Observer O measures the area 3 m$^2$ of an equilateral triangle ABC at rest in his xy-plane. An observer O′ moving relative to O at 0.6c observes the triangle A′B′C′. The triangle symmetry axis forms an angle $\theta = 15^{\circ}$ with respect to the direction x–x′ as measured by observer O What is the area measured by O′?
A pipe flowing light oil has a manometer attached. What is the absolute pressure in the pipe flow?

Consider the circuit shown in the figure. Switch \(S\) is closed at time \(t = 0\), causing a current \(i_1\) through the inductive branch and a current \(i_2\) through the capacitive branch. The initial charge on the capacitor is zero, and the charge at time \(t\) is \(q_2\). The total current through the battery is \(i = i_1 + i_2\). At what time \(t_2\) (accurate to two significant figures) will \(i\) equal one-half of its final value?

A 0.6 m diameter household fan takes air in at 98 kPa, 20\circ\text{C} and delivers it at 105 kPa, 21\circ\text{C} with a velocity of 1.5 m/s. What are the mass flow rate (kg/s), the inlet velocity and the outgoing volume flow rate in m^3/s?

A refrigerator using R-410a is powered by a small natural gas-fired heat engine with a thermal efficiency of \(25\%\). The R-410a condenses at \(40^\circ \mathrm{C}\) and evaporates at \(-20^\circ \mathrm{C}\), and the cycle is standard. What is the overall COP as \(Q_L / Q_1\)?

A car approaching a crossing C at a speed of 20 m/s sounds a horn of frequency 500 Hz. Speed of sound in air is 330 m/s. What frequency is heard by an observer (at rest) 60 m from the crossing on the straight road which crosses car road at right angles?

A metal bar with length \( L \), mass \( m \), and resistance \( R \) slides down frictionless inclined rails at angle \( \phi \) in a downward magnetic field \( \vec{B} \); the rails have negligible resistance. After the terminal speed has been reached, at what rate is electrical energy being converted to thermal energy in the resistance of the bar?

A vertical cylinder fitted with a piston contains $5\,\text{kg}$ of R-410a at $10\,^{\circ}\text{C}$, as shown in the figure. Heat is transferred to the system, causing the piston to rise until it reaches a set of stops, at which point the volume has doubled. Additional heat is transferred until the temperature inside reaches $50\,^{\circ}\text{C}$, at which point the pressure inside the cylinder is $1.4\,\text{MPa}$. Calculate the heat transfer for the overall process.

Sound waves with frequency 3000\,\text{Hz} and speed 343\,\text{m/s} diffract through the rectangular opening of a speaker cabinet and into a large auditorium of length \( d = 100\,\text{m} \). The opening, which has a horizontal width of 30.0\,\text{cm}, faces a wall 100\,\text{m} away. Along that wall, how far from the central axis will a listener be at the first diffraction minimum and thus have difficulty hearing the sound? (Neglect reflections.)
Figure shows a standing sound wave in a tube. The tube is filled with an unknown gas. What is the speed of sound in this gas?
A piston/cylinder has 0.5 kg of air at 2000 kPa, 1000 K. The cylinder has stops, so Vmin =0.03 m^3. The air now cools to 400 K by heat transfer to the ambient. Find the heat transfer.

An atomic beam is prepared by heating a collection of atoms to a temperature \( T \) and allowing them to emerge horizontally through a small hole (of atomic dimensions) of diameter \( D \) in one side of the oven. Estimate the diameter of the beam after it has travelled a horizontal length \( L \) along its path. The mass of an atom is \( M \).

Two loudspeakers, \( A \) and \( B \) (\textbf{figure}), are driven by the same amplifier and emit sinusoidal waves in phase. Consider point \( Q \) along the extension of the line connecting the speakers, \( 1.00\,\text{m} \) to the right of speaker \( B \). Both speakers emit sound waves that travel directly from the speaker to point \( Q \). What is the lowest frequency for which \textit{destructive} interference occurs at point \( Q \)?

A geothermal supply of hot water at 500 kPa, 150\circ\text{C} is fed to an insulated flash evaporator at the rate of 1.5 kg/s. A stream of saturated liquid at 200 kPa is drained from the bottom of the chamber and a stream of saturated vapor at 200 kPa is drawn from the top and fed to a turbine. Find the rate of entropy generation in the flash evaporator.

Water in a piston/cylinder is at 1 MPa, 500\circ\text{C}. There are two stops, a lower one at which Vmin = 1 m^3 and an upper one at Vmax = 3 m^3. The piston is loaded with a mass and outside atmosphere such that it floats when the pressure is 500 kPa. This setup is now cooled to 100\circ\text{C} by rejecting heat to the surrounding at 20\circ\text{C}. Find the total entropy generated in the process.

final_category_zh: 近代物理、量子与相对论
question: An electron at point \( A \) has a speed \( v_0 = 1.41 \times 10^6\,\text{m/s} \). Find the time required for the electron to move from A to B.

In an evacuated enclosure, a vertical cylindrical tank is sealed by a 3.00\,kg circular disk that can move up and down without friction. Beneath the disk is a quantity of ideal gas at temperature $T$ in the cylinder. Initially the disk is at rest at a distance of $h = 4.00$\,m above the bottom of the tank. When a lead brick of mass 9.00\,kg is gently placed on the disk, the disk moves downward. If the temperature of the gas is kept constant and no gas escapes from the tank, what distance abov...

A large, 50.0-cm-diameter metal cylinder filled with air supports a piston that can slide up and down without friction. The piston is 100.0 cm above the bottom when the temperature is 20^\circ C. A student then stands on the piston. After several minutes have elapsed, by how much has the piston been depressed?
What is the electric potential at point C? What is the electric potential at point C?

To refresh air in a room, a counterflow heat exchanger is mounted in the wall, drawing in outside air at \(0.5^\circ \mathrm{C},\ 80\%\) relative humidity and pushing out room air at \(40^\circ \mathrm{C},\ 50\%\) relative humidity. Assume an exchange of \(3\,\mathrm{kg/min}\) dry air in a steady-flow device, and also assume that the room air exits the heat exchanger at \(23^\circ \mathrm{C}\) to the outside. Find the \(\phi\) for the fresh air entering the room.
A resistor \(X\) is connected to three other resistors. An ohmmeter connected across \(a\) and \(b\) reads \(2.00\,\Omega\). What is the resistance of \(X\)?

A block of volume $1 \, \text{L}$ is heat treated at $500 \, ^{\circ}\text{C}$ and now cooled in a $200 \, \text{L}$ oil bath initially at $20 \, ^{\circ}\text{C}$, as shown in figure. Assuming no heat transfer with the surroundings, what is the final temperature?

A 280 Hz sound wave is directed into one end of the trombone slide. A microphone is placed at the other end to record the intensity of sound waves that are transmitted through the tube. What is the smallest slide extensions \text{s} that will make the microphone detect a maximum of sound intensity?

A $1 \, \text{m}^3$ rigid tank has propane at $100 \, \text{kPa}$, $300 \, \text{K}$ and connected by a valve to another tank of $0.5 \, \text{m}^3$ with propane at $250 \, \text{kPa}$, $400 \, \text{K}$. The valve is opened, and the two tanks come to a uniform state at $325 \, \text{K}$. What is the final pressure?
A laser beam is incident on the left mirror in Figure. Its initial direction is parallel to a line that bisects the mirrors. What is the angle \phi of the reflected laser beam?
In the circuit, both batteries have insignificant internal resistance and the idealized ammeter reads \(1.50\,\text{A}\) in the direction shown. Find the emf \(\mathcal{E}\) of the battery.

A flow of moist air from a domestic furnace, state 1, is at \(45^\circ \mathrm{C}\), \(10\%\) relative humidity with a flow rate of \(0.05\, \mathrm{kg/s}\) dry air. A small electric heater adds steam at \(100^\circ \mathrm{C},\, 100\, \mathrm{kPa}\) generated from tap water at \(15^\circ \mathrm{C}\). Up in the living room the flow comes out at state 4: \(30^\circ \mathrm{C},\, 60\%\) relative humidity. Find the power needed for the electric heater.

A cylindrical shell with radius \( R_1 \), height \( H \), and charge \( Q_1 \) rotates with angular speed \( \omega_1 \). A small charged disk of radius \( R_2 \), mass \( M \), and charge \( Q_2 \) spins with angular velocity \( \omega_2 \) at an angle \( \theta \) to the axis and precesses with angular velocity \( \Omega \). The magnetic moment of the disk has magnitude \( \mu = \frac{1}{4} Q_2 \omega_2 R_2^2 \). What is the magnitude of the torque exerted on the disk?

Three light waves combine at a certain point where their electric field components are \( E_1 = E_0 \sin \omega t, \quad E_2 = E_0 \sin(\omega t + 60^\circ), \quad E_3 = E_0 \sin(\omega t - 30^\circ) \). Find their resultant component \( E(t) \) at that point.
A heat engine using a monatomic gas follows the cycle shown in figure. What is the thermal efficiency of this heat engine?

At time zero, the oscillator passes point \( A \) moving to the right. At \( t = 2\,\mathrm{s} \), it reaches point \( B \) for the first time. It is known that the oscillator has equal speed magnitudes when passing points \( A \) and \( B \), and the total distance traveled within the 2 seconds is \( 5.6\,\mathrm{m} \). What is the amplitude?

We wish to produce refrigeration at−30\circ\text{C}\. A reservoir is available at 200\circ\text{C}\ and the ambient temperature is 30\circ\text{C}.Thus, work can be done by a cyclic heat engine operating between the 200\circ\text{C} reservoir and the ambient. This work is used to drive the refrigerator. Determine the ratio of the heat transferred from the 200\circ\text{C}\ reservoir to the heat transferred from the−30\circ\text{C} reservoir, assuming all processes are reversible.

An ideal diatomic gas has a molar internal energy equal to \( E = \frac{5}{2}RT \) which depends only on its absolute temperature \( T \). A mole of this gas is taken quasi\-statically first from state \( A \) to state \( B \), and then from state \( B \) to state \( C \) along the straight line paths shown in the diagram of pressure \( p \) versus volume \( V \). What is the work done by the gas in the process \( A \rightarrow B \rightarrow C \)?

An emergency drain pump should be able to pump 0.1 m^3/s liquid water at 15\circ\text{C}, 10 m vertically up, delivering it with a velocity of 20 m/s. It is estimated that the pump, pipe, and nozzle have a combined isentropic efficiency expressed for the pump as 60%. How much power is needed to drive the pump?

Two branches are connected in parallel and then joined through a \(7.00\Omega\) resistor. The left branch contains a \(24.0\text{V}\) battery and a \(3.00\Omega\) resistor, and the right branch contains a battery of emf \(\mathcal{E}\) and a \(2.00\Omega\) resistor. All batteries have negligible internal resistance. What is the value of the emf \(\mathcal{E}\) such that no current flows through the \(7.00\Omega\) resistor?

Figure shows the path taken by a knot in a jet of ionized gas that has been expelled from a galaxy. The knot travels at constant velocity at angle $\theta$ from the direction of Earth. The knot occasionally emits a burst of light, which is eventually detected on Earth. Two bursts are indicated in the figure, separated by time $t$ as measured in a stationary frame near the bursts. The apparent distance traveled by the knot between the two bursts is the distance across an Earth-observer's view...

A 1-m$^3$ rigid tank has propane at 100 kPa, 300 K and connected by a valve to another tank of 0.5 m$^3$ with propane at 250 kPa, 400 K. The valve is opened, and the two tanks come to a uniform state at 325 K. What is the final pressure?

A heat pump is used to heat a house during the winter. The house is to be maintained at 20\circ\text{C} at all times. When the ambient temperature outside drops to−10\circ\text{C}, the rate at which heat is lost from the house is estimated to be 25 kW. What is the minimum electrical power required to drive the heat pump?
A heat engine using \( 1.0\,\mathrm{mol} \) of a monatomic gas follows the cycle shown in the figure. What is the thermal efficiency of this heat engine?

A capacitor with \( C = 20.0\mu\text{F} \) is initially charged to \( 100\text{V} \) and then discharges through a resistor \( R_0 = 10.0\Omega \) when the switch is closed at \( t = 0 \); a nearby rectangular loop with dimensions \( a = 10.0\text{cm} \), \( b = 20.0\text{cm} \), placed \( c = 5.0\text{cm} \) from the wire, and having 25 turns of wire with resistance \( 1.0\Omega/\text{m} \), is affected by the magnetic field from the discharging current. Find the current in the small circuit...
As shown in figure How far down the slope does the arrow hit if it is shot with a speed of 50 m/s from 1.75 m above the ground?

A piston/cylinder assembly contains $2 \, \text{kg}$ of liquid water at $20 \, ^{\circ}\text{C}$ and $300 \, \text{kPa}$, as shown in figure. There is a linear spring mounted on the piston such that when the water is heated, the pressure reaches $3 \, \text{MPa}$ with a volume of $0.1 \, \text{m}^3$. Find the work in the process.

Electrons are continually being knocked out of air molecules in the atmosphere by cosmic-ray particles coming in from space. Once released, each electron experiences an electric force \( \vec{F} \) due to the electric field \( \vec{E} \) that is produced in the atmosphere by charged particles already on Earth. Near Earth's surface the electric field has the magnitude \( E = 150\ \mathrm{N/C} \) and is directed downward. What is the change \( \Delta U \) in the electric potential energy of a r...

An engine, shown in figure, consists of a $100 \, \text{kg}$ cast iron block with a $20 \, \text{kg}$ aluminum head, $20 \, \text{kg}$ of steel parts, $5 \, \text{kg}$ of engine oil, and $6 \, \text{kg}$ of glycerine (antifreeze). All initial temperatures are $5 \, ^{\circ}\text{C}$, and as the engine starts we want to know how hot it becomes if it absorbs a net of $7000 \, \text{kJ}$ before it reaches a steady uniform temperature.

The graph in \textbf{figure} shows a $pV$-diagram of the air in a human lung when a person is inhaling and then exhaling a deep breath. Such graphs, obtained in clinical practice, are normally somewhat curved, but we have modeled one as a set of straight lines of the same general shape. \textit{(Important: The pressure shown is the \textit{gauge} pressure, \textit{not} the absolute pressure.)} The process illustrated here is somewhat different from those we have been studying, because the pre...

A 5-kg piston in a cylinder with a diameter of 100 mm is loaded with a linear spring and the outside atmospheric pressure is 100 kPa. The spring exerts no force on the piston when it is at the bottom of the cylinder, and for the state shown, the pressure is 400 kPa with volume 0.4 L. The valve is opened to let some air in, causing the piston to rise 2 cm. Find the new pressure.

In a cogenerating steam power plant, the turbine receives steam from a high-pressure drum and from a low-pressure drum. For the turbine calculation, assume a mass-weighted average entropy and neglect entropy generation by mixing. The condenser consists of two closed heat exchangers used for district heating. The high-temperature heater and the low-temperature heater add thermal energy to the heating water. Find the temperature in the return line to the deaerator.

A man \( 1.80 \, \mathrm{m} \) tall stands in front of a mirror and sees his full height, no more and no less. If his eyes are \( 0.14 \, \mathrm{m} \) from the top of his head, what is the minimum height of the mirror?

A rubber ball is dropped onto a ramp that is tilted at 20^\circ , as shown in Figure. A bouncing ball obeys the “law of reflection,” which says that the ball leaves the surface at the same angle it approached the surface. What is the ball’s rebound speed on its first bounce?

final_category_zh: 近代物理、量子与相对论
question: Figure shows the decay of parents in a radioactive sample. The axes are scaled by $N_s = 2.00 \times 10^6$ and $t_s = 10.0 \, \text{s}$. What is the activity of the sample at $t = 27.0 \, \text{s}$?

The \( pV \)-diagram in \textbf{figure} shows a cycle of a heat engine that uses 0.250 mol of an ideal gas with \( \gamma = 1.40 \). Process \( ab \) is adiabatic. (a) Find the pressure of the gas at point \( a \). What is the thermal efficiency of the engine?

A counterflowing heat exchanger has one line with 2 kg/s at 125 kPa, 1000 K entering, and the air is leaving at 100 kPa, 400 K. The other line has 0.5 kg/s water coming in at 200 kPa, 20\circ\text{C} and leaving at 200 kPa. What is the total rate of entropy generation?

In the figure, a beam of monochromatic light reflects and refracts at point \(A\) on the interface between material 1 with index of refraction \(n_1 = 1.33\) and material 2 with index of refraction \(n_2 = 1.77\). What is the angle of refraction at point \(A\)?
0.10 mol of nitrogen gas undergoes the two processes shown in the figure. How much heat is required for process B (the isobaric expansion)?

A bar with mass \( M = 1.20\ \text{kg} \) and resistance \( R = 0.500\ \Omega \) slides on a U-shaped rail of width \( W = 40.0\ \text{cm} \), connected to a spring with \( k = 90.0\ \text{N/m} \); it is released from rest at \( x = 10.0\ \text{cm} \) in a \( 1.00\ \text{T} \) magnetic field directed into the page. What is the amplitude of the motion at time \( t = 5.00\ \text{s} \)?

The three ropes in Figure are tied to a small, very light ring. Two of the ropes are anchored to walls at right angles, and the third rope pulls as shown. What is T2, the magnitude of the tension forces in the second rope?

Suppose the moving rod in figure is \( 0.10 \\ \mathrm{m} \) long, the velocity \( v \) is \( 2.5 \\ \mathrm{m/s} \), the total resistance of the loop is \( 0.030 \\ \Omega \), and \( B \) is \( 0.60 \\ \mathrm{T} \). Find the force acting on the rod.

Consider two tanks, $A$ and $B$, connected by a valve, as shown in the figure. Each has a volume of $200 \, \text{L}$, and tank $A$ has R-410a at $25 \, ^{\circ}\text{C}$, with $10\%$ liquid and $90\%$ vapor by volume, while tank $B$ is evacuated. The valve is now opened, and saturated vapor flows from $A$ to $B$ until the pressure in $B$ has reached that in $A$, at which point the valve is closed. This process occurs slowly such that all temperatures stay at $25 \, ^{\circ}\text{C}$ througho...

Figure is an energy-level diagram for a fictitious infinite potential well that contains one electron. The number of degenerate states of the levels are indicated. We put a total of 11 electrons in the well. If the electrostatic forces between the electrons can be neglected, what multiple of \( h^2 \!/ 8mL^2 \) gives the energy of the first excited state of the 11-electron system?

An adiabatic steam turbine in a power plant receives 5 kg/s steam at 3000 kPa, 500\circ\text{C}. Twenty percent of the flow is extracted at 1000 kPa, 350\circ\text{C} to a feedwater heater, and the remainder flows out at 200 kPa, 200\circ\text{C}. Find the turbine power output.
Two point charges are placed on the \( x \)-axis as shown. Find the position on the \( x \)-axis where the net force on a small charge \( +q \) would be zero.

A person with compromised pinch strength in his fingers can exert a force of only 6.0 N to either side of a pinch-held object, such as the book shown in Figure. The coefficient of static friction between his fingers and the book cover is 0.80. What is the heaviest book he can hold vertically before it slips out of his fingers?
The mirror in figure deflects a horizontal laser beam What is the angle \phi?

A 1-m^3 rigid tank contains 100 kg R-410a at a temperature of 15\circ\text{C}. A valve on top of the tank is opened, and saturated vapor is throttled to ambient pressure, 100 kPa and flows to a collector system. During the process, the temperature inside the tank remains at 15\circ\text{C} by heat transfer from the 20\circ\text{C} ambient. The valve is closed when no more liquid remains inside. Calculate the total entropy generation in the process.

Solid carbon is burned with stoichiometric air in a steady-flow process. The reactants at T_0, P_0 are heated in a preheater to T_2 = 500\ \mathrm{K}, as shown in figure, with the energy given by the product gases before flowing to a second heat exchanger, which they leave at T_0. Find the heat transfer per kmol of fuel (4 to 5) in the second heat exchanger.

A coaxial cable consists of an insulated wire carrying current \( I_1 = 3.00 \, \mathrm{A} \) surrounded by a cylindrical conductor carrying current \( I_2 = 1.00 \, \mathrm{A} \) in the opposite direction, as in figure. Calculate the magnetic field outside the cylindrical conductor at \( r_{\mathrm{ext}} = 1.50 \, \mathrm{cm} \).

A problem of practical interest is to make a beam of electrons turn a \( 90^\circ \) corner. This can be done with the parallel-plate capacitor shown in figure. An electron with kinetic energy \( 3.0 imes 10^{-17}\\,\\mathrm{J} \) enters through a small hole in the bottom plate of the capacitor. What strength electric field is needed if the electron is to emerge from an exit hole \( 1.0\\,\\mathrm{cm} \) away from the entrance hole, traveling at right angles to its original direction?
The batteries shown in the circuit have negligibly small internal resistances. Find the current through the 10.0 V battery.

Consider the neutron capture reaction \[{}^{109}\mathrm{Ag} + n \rightarrow {}^{110}\mathrm{Ag} \rightarrow {}^{110}\mathrm{Ag} + \gamma\], in which a compound nucleus (\({}^{110}\mathrm{Ag}\)) is formed. The figure shows the relative rate at which such events take place, plotted against the energy of the incoming neutron. Here \(\Delta E\) is a measure of the uncertainty with which the energy of a state can be defined. The quantity \(\Delta t\) is a measure of the time available to measure t...

A two-stage air compressor has an intercooler between the two stages, as shown in figure. The inlet state is \(100\,\mathrm{kPa},\ 290\,\mathrm{K}\), and the final exit pressure is \(1.6\,\mathrm{MPa}\). Assume that the constant-pressure intercooler cools the air to the inlet temperature, \(T_3 = T_1\). It can be shown that the optimal pressure is \(P_2 = (P_1 P_4)^{1/2}\) for minimum total compressor work. Find the intercooler heat transfer for the optimal \(P_2\).
Water at 1000 kPa, 250\circ\text{C} is brought to saturated vapor in a rigid container. Find the final temperature in this isometric process.

A cylindrical glass rod (figure) has index of refraction 1.52. It is surrounded by water that has index of refraction of 1.33. One end is ground to a hemispherical surface. A small object is placed on the axis of the rod to the left of the vertex. Find the lateral magnification.

A $1 \, \text{m}^3$ rigid tank with air at $1 \, \text{MPa}$ and $400 \, \text{K}$ is connected to an air line as shown in figure. The valve is opened and air flows into the tank until the pressure reaches $5 \, \text{MPa}$, at which point the valve is closed and the temperature inside is $450 \, \text{K}$. The tank eventually cools to room temperature, $300 \, \text{K}$. What is the pressure inside the tank then?
The figure shows two uniformly charged spheres. How much higher is b's potential compared to a?

A Jurassic mosquito is discovered embedded in a chunk of amber, which has index of refraction 1.6. One surface of the amber is spherically convex with radius of curvature 3.0 mm. The mosquito's head happens to be on the central axis of that surface and, when viewed along the axis, appears to be buried 5.0 mm into the amber. How deep is it really?

A string fixed at both ends has consecutive standing wave modes for which the distances between adjacent nodes are 18 cm and 16 cm, respectively. If the tension is 10 N and the linear mass density is 4 g/m, what is the fundamental frequency?
Assume that 2 kg/s of hot air at 2000 K and 500 kPa is mixed with 1.5 kg/s air at 500 K, 500 kPa without any external heat transfer. Find the exit temperature.
The figure shows a snapshot graph of a wave traveling. At this instant, what is the velocity of points 1 on the string?

An electric dipole is centered at the origin, with \( \vec{p} \) in the direction of the \( +y \)-axis. Derive an approximate expression for the electric field at a point \( P \) on the \( y \)-axis for which \( y \) is much larger than \( d \). To do this, use the binomial expansion \( (1 + x)^n \approx 1 + nx + n(n - 1)x^2/2 + \dots \) (valid for the case \( |x| < 1 \)).

The machine in Figure has an 800 g steel shuttle that is pulled along a square steel rail by an elastic cord. The shuttle is released when the elastic cord has 20 N tension at a 45 angle. What is the initial acceleration of the shuttle?

A submerged scuba diver hears the sound of a boat horn directly above her on the surface of the lake. At the same time, a friend on dry land also hears the horn (figure). The horn is \(1.2\,\text{m}\) above the surface of the water. Both air and water are at \(20^{\circ}\text{C}\). What is the distance (labeled "?") from the horn to the diver?

A garage door is mounted on an overhead rail. The wheels at \( A \) and \( B \) have rusted so that they do not roll, but rather slide along the track. The coefficient of kinetic friction is \( 0.52 \). Each wheel is \( 0.50\, \text{m} \) from the vertical sides of the door. The door is uniform and weighs \( 950\, \text{N} \). It is pushed to the left at constant speed by a horizontal force \( \vec{F} \), that is applied as shown in the figure. Find the maximum value \( h \) can have without...
The electric potential at the dot in figure is 3140 V. What is charge q?

Figure gives the velocity $u'$ of a particle relative to frame $S'$ for a range of values of $v$, where $v$ is the velocity of frame $S'$ relative to a reference frame $S$. The vertical axis scale is set by $u'_a = -0.800c$. What value will $u'$ have if $v \rightarrow c$?

In figure, Sally (at point \( A \)) and Sam's spaceship (of proper length \( L_0 = 230 \, \mathrm{m} \)) pass each other with constant relative speed \( v \). Sally measures a time interval of \( 3.57 \, \mu\mathrm{s} \) for the ship to pass her. In terms of \( c \), what is the relative speed \( v \) between Sally and the ship?

A cylindrical glass rod (figure) has index of refraction 1.52. It is surrounded by air. One end is ground to a hemispherical surface. A small object is placed on the axis of the rod to the left of the vertex. Find the lateral magnification.

A plane sinusoidal transverse wave has a speed \( v = 2\,\mathrm{m/s} \). The wave profile at \( t = 0 \) is shown in the figure. At \( t = 0 \), the displacement of particle \( A \), whose equilibrium position is at the origin, is \( y = \sqrt{2}\,\mathrm{cm} \). What is the wave length?

A U-shaped tube open to the air at both ends contains some mercury. A quantity of water is carefully poured into the left arm of the U-shaped tube. Calculate the vertical distance \( h \) from the top of the mercury in the right-hand arm of the tube to the top of the water in the left-hand arm.

The conducting box in figure has been given an excess negative charge. The surface density of excess electrons at the center of the top surface is \( 5.0 imes 10^{10} \) electrons/m\(^2\). What are the electric field strengths \( E_1 \) to \( E_3 \) at points 1 to 3?

In the circuit, an NPN transistor obeys the relations \( I_C = \beta I_B \), \( I_E = I_C + I_B \), and \( V_{\text{in}} - V_e = 0.60\,\text{V} \), where \( \beta \gg 1 \). If the input potential includes a small time-dependent signal, so that \( V_{\text{in}} = 15.0\,\text{V} + v_{\text{in}}(t) \), then the output potential is \( V_{\text{out}} = 7.5\,\text{V} + G v_{\text{in}}(t) \), where \( G \) is a gain factor. What is \( G \) for this circuit?

You are standing 2.5 m directly in front of one of the two loudspeakers. The room temperature is 20 \circ\text{C}. As you begin to walk directly away from the speaker, what is the smallest distances from the speaker do you hear a minimum sound intensity?

The figure shows the light intensity on a screen 2.5 m behind an aperture. The aperture is illuminated with light of wave length 600 nm. If the aperture is a double slit, what is the spacing between the slits?

A $10\,\text{m}$-high cylinder, with a cross-sectional area of $0.1\,\text{m}^2$, has a massless piston at the bottom with water at $20\,^{\circ}\text{C}$ on top of it, as shown in the figure. Air at $300\,\text{K}$, with a volume of $0.3\,\text{m}^3$, under the piston is heated so that the piston moves up, spilling the water out over the side. Find the total heat transfer to the air when all the water has been pushed out.

A piston/cylinder device keeping a constant pressure has 1 kg water at 20\circ\text{C} and 1 kg of water at 100\circ\text{C}, both at 500 kPa separated by a thin membrane. The membrane is broken and the water comes to a uniform state, with no external heat transfer. Find the entropy generation for the process.
As shown in the figure, the spring constant such that one single spring connected at the centre produces an equivalent effect on the two springs is:

From point P in the center of the open side, a laser is pointed at the mirrored wall. At what angle of incidence must the light strike the right-hand wall so that, after being reflected, the light hits the left corner of the back wall?

The figure shows the velocity graph of a particle moving along the x-axis. Its initial position is \( x_0 = 2.0\ \mathrm{m} \) at \( t_0 = 0\ \mathrm{s} \). At \( t = 2.0\ \mathrm{s} \), what are the particle’s acceleration?

Air in a piston/cylinder arrangement is at 200 kPa, 300 K with a volume of 0.5 m^3. If the piston is at the stops, the volume is 1 m^3 and a pressure of 400 kPa is required. The air is then heated from the initial state to 1500 K by a 1700 K reservoir. Find the total irreversibility in the process, assuming the surroundings are at 20\circ\text{C}.

The water molecule \(\mathrm{H_2O}\) has a permanent dipole moment of magnitude \(6.2\times10^{-30}\,\mathrm{C\,m}\). A water molecule is located some distance from a \(\mathrm{Na^+}\) ion in a saltwater solution. What force does the ion exert on the water molecule?
